motions.infoslide: For the purposes of this debate, military leaders would still be subject to the authority of elected civilian leaders.

motions.motion: That in the aftermath of the repeal of Section 377A, the LGBTQ+ movement in Singapore should actively prioritise fighting for reforms that impact the Trans community, at the exclusion of fighting for gay marriage.
motions.infoslide: The repeal of Section 377A was a landmark victory for the LGBTQ+ movement in Singapore, marking the culmination of a decade of advocacy. Section 377A had criminalised consensual same-sex relations between men. The success of its repeal, while celebrated, was not a complete triumph - the government also amended the constitution to prevent legal challenges to the definition of marriage as between a man and a woman.

motions.motion: That the Philippines should disallow split-ticket voting.
motions.infoslide: Currently, the Philippines allows 'split-ticket' voting where the President and Vice President are elected separately, rather than together (such as in the United States and Indonesia). Since the 1987 Constitution of the Philippines, only two elections have resulted in the President and Vice Presidents being elected from the same political party: in 2004, President Gloria Macapagal-Arroyo with running mate VP Noli De Castro, and in 2022, President Bongbong Marcos with running mate VP Sara Duterte. Traditionally, Vice Presidents in the Philippines are appointed as secretaries of departments (e.g. Department of Social Welfare and Development, Education, and Agriculture) under the executive.

motions.motion: That the US should abolish state by state incorporation and have businesses incorporated at the federal level.
motions.infoslide: In the US, corporate law varies substantially from state to state. Corporations can incorporate themselves in whatever state they choose and are then subject to the corporate law of that state. Under a national incorporation system, businesses would be incorporated at the federal level and be subject to one consistent set of federal corporate law.

motions.motion: That we oppose risk retention requirements.
motions.infoslide: Risk retention requirements require financial institutions which create and sell securities to investors to retain some portion of the credit risk associated with the securities they sell. Banks will continue to hold a portion of the assets they have sold to investors in order to expose themselves to that credit risk.

motions.motion: That China should require shadow banks to obtain formal bank licenses in order to continue operating.
motions.infoslide: Formal Chinese banks, most of which are state owned, operate under heavy regulations. As a result, a 'shadow banking' sector worth approximately US$3 trillion has emerged. ‘Shadow banks’ include trusts, asset managers and microfinance firms. These shadow banks partake in simple banking activities without formal banking licences (e.g. providing loans distinct from the regulated loans given by formal Chinese banks). For the purposes of this debate, forcing shadow banks to obtain a formal banking licence would require them to obtain the same licence as the formal Chinese banks.

motions.motion: That sports clubs should adopt a blanket 'no fault stand-down' policy.
motions.infoslide: Under a 'no fault stand-down' policy, any player charged with a serious criminal offence would be automatically suspended with full pay, pending the trial's outcome. The policy makes no judgement about the player's guilt, but operates as a precautionary blanket policy. It contrasts to a discretionary system, where each time a player is charged with a crime, the club makes a decision on whether to stand them down based on the circumstances.

motions.motion: That, as an artist, we would prioritise making our art comprehensible to audiences, at the expense of authentic self-expression.
That artists should always refuse requests to do unpaid work.
That we oppose the establishment of "prohibited art" museums.
motions.infoslide: For the purposes of this debate, a "prohibited art" museum is a museum specially curated to display works of art that were denounced or censored by the mainstream for political, social, or religious reasons. One example is the Museum of Prohibited Art that opened in Barcelona in October 2023, which houses exhibits such as "Piss Christ" (a blood-red photograph of the crucifixion immersed in the artist’s urine; Andres Serrano), "Silence" (an exhibit of 30 pairs of stiletto heels on the same number of Islamic prayer rugs; Zoulikha Bouabdellah) and "Shark" (a sculpture of a tied up, nearly- naked Saddam Hussein submerged in formaldehyde; David Cerny).

motions.motion: That we oppose the use of video refereeing to make on-field decisions.
That, as a reigning F2 champion with no promised F1 seat, we would choose to race in a different series over taking a F1 reserve seat.
motions.infoslide: Formula 2 is a 'spec series' (where drivers race identical cars) and is widely considered a training ground for Formula 1. Under current regulations, the F2 drivers' champion is not permitted to participate in another season of F2. Seats in F1 are increasingly hard to come by, with prior experience and sponsorship backing being a large factor in hiring decisions. Consequently, the three most recent F2 champions had to choose between a reserve seat with an F1 team (a role that entails working in the team’s driving simulator to develop the car, occasional test driving, and replacing a driver if they are sick or injured) or racing in a different series.

motions.motion: That we believe that the morally righteous choice is for the man to save the baby
motions.infoslide: A man enters a burning building on the verge of collapse. He sees a 25 year old mother and her only child, a newborn baby. Both are strangers to him. He can certainly rescue either the mother or her baby, but not both.

motions.motion: That we prefer a standard of strict liability to a standard of negligence in workplace harassment cases
motions.infoslide: In workplace harassment cases, strict liability holds employers accountable for harassment between employees, regardless of what steps were taken to prevent or address the harassment, while negligence requires proving the employer failed to take reasonable steps.

motions.motion: That we prefer a Tournament of Judges model for appointing judges to higher courts to a system of appointments by executives
motions.infoslide: A “Tournament of Judges” model is where judges compete for promotion to higher courts. This is based on objective metrics such as publication rates, citations by other courts and judges, speed of dispositions, and successful appeal rates.

motions.motion: That courts should impose adverse cost orders on parties who refuse reasonable settlement offers and subsequently fail to obtain a better outcome at trial
That India should aggressively pursue the development of SEZs
motions.infoslide: Special Economic Zones (SEZs) are geographically designated areas within a country that operate under reduced economic regulations. These include tax incentives, lower tariff rates and special customs procedures.

motions.motion: That we believe artificial intelligence will do more harm than good to the average person
That it is in the interests of the Asian Tigers to aggressively crackdown on unpaid overtime
That it is in the interests of the United States to significantly raise personal income and corporate taxes to reduce the budget deficit, rather than significantly reducing government spending
That Zohran Mamdani's victory in the New York mayoral primary is in the interest of the Democratic Party
That we oppose court-mandated Men's Behaviour Change programs
motions.infoslide: Men’s Behaviour Change Programs are government-run initiatives designed to combat gendered violence. Men in these programs meet regularly with a trained facilitator or counsellor in a group setting. The sessions focus on addressing issues such as unresolved childhood trauma, emotional regulation, substance abuse and the impact of past experiences on present behaviour. Men can attend voluntarily, or men convicted of an offence can be compelled to attend by court order.

motions.motion: That we oppose bans on sex-selective abortions
That it is in girls' interests to be sent to single-sex schools
That we should incentivise the construction of build-to-rent housing
motions.infoslide: Build-to-Rent (BTR) refers to residential developments that are built primarily to be rented out, rather than sold to individual buyers. Typically, BTR properties are owned and managed by a single institutional landlord, such as a property fund, superannuation fund, or real estate investment trust.

motions.motion: That, in developed states, we support governments significantly investing in housing construction under the Singaporean model
motions.infoslide: In Singapore, Housing & Development Board (HDB) units are public housing units built and managed by the government. Under this scheme, residents purchase the lease, not the land. After 99 years, the unit reverts to the state.

motions.motion: That, in cities facing housing crises, we would ban individuals from purchasing more than two existing (i.e. not newly built) dwellings
That states which have recently formed through religious secession should establish Religious Councils rather than implementing secularism
motions.infoslide: Religious Councils are councils of religious leaders from the dominant faith in a country which advise governments on whether their laws comply with religious scripture before they are passed. Their advice is non-binding.

motions.motion: That we support states employing surrendered militants for counter-insurgency measures
That the Indian feminist movement should actively encourage women to participate in the Agniveer scheme
motions.infoslide: The Agniveer scheme, launched by the Indian government, is a short-term military recruitment program for youth aged 17.5 to 21. Recruits serve in the armed forces for four years, after which 25% are selected on merit for long- term service. The remaining receive a one-time severance package but no pension or permanent benefits.

motions.motion: That MERCOSUR countries should allow the free movement of labour amongst member states
motions.infoslide: MERCOSUR is a trade bloc in South America including Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay and Uruguay.

motions.motion: That, as a West African nation, we would offer diaspora (including descendants of enslaved persons) citizenship and tax incentives to return to their home nation
That it is in the interest of the al-Sharaa Syrian government to sign the Abraham Accords
That, when a loved one disappears for months without explanation, we would rather believe they chose to leave than believe they were dead
That parents from low socioeconomic backgrounds should teach their children to believe happiness is the only thing in life that really matters
That US universities should comply with the Trump administration
That it is in PGA Tour's interests to make a large investment in virtual golf arenas and include virtual golf tournaments on the PGA Tour
motions.infoslide: PGA Tour is a non-profit organisation that runs 'The PGA Tour', its flagship series of North American Golf Tournaments. It is largely player run, with roughly half of its board being current players, advised by a player-elected Player Advisory Council (PAC). Virtual golf arenas are large indoor entertainment centres. In Phase 1, players tee-off towards a virtual golf simulator, and play through the hole virtually until reaching the green. They then complete the hole on an in-arena putting green.

motions.motion: That, as an English Premier League football club outside of the Big Six, we would prioritise signing young talents on long-term deals over buying established stars
That we support referees and refereeing bodies having a bias toward star sports players
That we prefer the New Zealand UE system to a system where entry to university degrees is granted on high school marks and university entrance exam results
motions.infoslide: In New Zealand, University Entrance (UE) is the minimum grade required for admission to university and does not change year on year. UE allows students entry into any university course and there is no limit on the amount of spaces offered. Entrance into specialised courses with limited spaces (e.g. medicine, law, engineering majors) is determined by results in your first year at university (e.g. first-year Health Sciences papers for entry into Medicine). If you do not get into the specialised course you apply for, the first-year papers will be cross-credited to another relevant degree (e.g. Bachelor of Health Sciences).

motions.motion: That the United States National Institutes of Health (NIH) should limit the amount of grant money universities can use for overhead expenses (e.g. administration, utilities) to no more than 15% of research expenses (e.g. researcher salaries, equipment, facilities)
motions.infoslide: Universities receiving National Institutes of Health (NIH) research grants typically receive approximately 50% of direct research costs as additional funding to cover overhead expenses such as administration and utilities.

motions.motion: That universities should be required to cover 50% of the unpaid student loan debt when a student fails to graduate and subsequently defaults on their loan

That, as drug reform advocates in western liberal democracies, we would prioritise progressive public health narratives (e.g. harm minimisation) over libertarian civil rights narratives (e.g. bodily freedom) when discussing party drugs
motions.infoslide: For the purpose of this debate, party drugs are cocaine, ecstasy (MDMA), ketamine, LSD and GHB.

motions.motion: That, in countries where cannabis is being legalised, we would do so using a state monopoly model (i.e. state-run cannabis outlets) rather than a commercial model
That we support involuntary inpatient treatment (i.e. long to medium term stays in psychiatric facilities or rehabilitation centres) for drug addicts
That we support the decline of Traditional News outlets in favour of New Media
motions.infoslide: New Media outlets are characterised by their relatively small size and focus on online distribution (e.g. podcasting, social media commentating and blog- style posting). Examples include Breitbart, Rebel News, Turning Point USA, Vox, Hasan Piker, The Daily Aus, etc. Traditional Media refers to established mastheads, which are generally larger in size and tend to distribute in a variety of mediums (e.g. in print, online and on social media). Examples include The New York Times, The Australian Financial Review, Fox News, Al Jazeera, the BBC, Chosun Ilbo, etc.
motions.round: English as a Foreign Language Finals

motions.motion: That we would hold political party leadership accountable for crimes committed by candidates during elections and elected members whilst in office
motions.round: English as a Foreign Language Finals

motions.motion: That we prefer a world where people are meaningfully less inclined to backlash against protests which turn violent
motions.round: English as a Foreign Language Finals

motions.motion: That we would abolish the Price Limit Rule in China’s Stock Exchanges
motions.infoslide: The Price Limit Rule is a stock market regulation in China that restricts the daily price movement of individual stocks within a fixed percentage range. When a stock reaches the upper limit or lower limit, further trading at a higher or lower price is not allowed for the rest of the trading day.
motions.round: English as a Second Language Finals

motions.motion: That we support Southeast Asian nations privatising state owned banks
motions.round: English as a Second Language Finals

motions.motion: That, in times of recession, we should prohibit publicly traded companies engaged in critical sectors (e.g. health, security, energy) from going private
motions.round: English as a Second Language Finals

motions.motion: That we support left-wing parties adopting nationalist environmental policy and rhetoric (e.g. economic protectionism for renewable energy projects, portraying environmental damage as a threat to national safety, portraying the green transition as necessary for energy security and independence)

That it is in the interests of Pacific Island nations threatened by sea level rise to prioritise purchasing land from neighbouring developing countries for the purpose of future relocation, over negotiating pathways to permanent residence in developed countries
That we oppose the reopening of the Panguna mine
motions.infoslide: Bougainville is a semi-autonomous region of Papua New Guinea. The Panguna Copper mine was opened in 1972 by Bougainville Copper Limited (BCL). Local concerns about the mine revived a secessionist movement, leading to the mine’s sabotage in 1989 and a civil war that killed 20,000 people. A 2001 peace deal granted autonomy and promised a referendum, which in 2019 saw 98% vote for independence. The mine remains uncontained, with an estimated $80 billion in copper and gold. The Autonomous Bougainville Government now owns 72% of BCL, and is considering reopening the mine as part of its 2027 independence plan.

motions.motion: THR the decline of Franchise Loyalty in professional team sports
motions.infoslide: For the purposes of this debate, "Franchise Loyalty" refers to players prioritizing to stay in the same team for an extended period of time.

motions.motion: THW disallow district level courts from issuing nationwide injunctions in the United States of America
motions.infoslide: In the USA, nationwide injunctions are court orders that bind the federal government, even in its relations with non-parties in a lawsuit, to stop enforcing a law or regulation. It stops the government from acting in a particular way nationwide, regardless of the specific geographic location of the lawsuit.

motions.motion: THP the university-governance system over the researcher-governance system within universities
motions.infoslide: There are two systems for corporate sponsorship of research in universities. In a university-governance system, the university manages the commercialization process, with companies providing large-scale grants and partnering directly with the institution, which then respectively distributes funding to researchers. In contrast, in a researcher-governance system, companies fund researchers directly through giving grants to specific research or hiring and compensating faculty while they continue to operate within the university.

motions.motion: THW mandate real-term (i.e. adjusted for inflation) funding cuts for tertiary education programmes whose graduates have below-median tax contributions five years after graduation
TH, as parents of an academically gifted child, W choose not to grade-skip their child at any grade level
THW ban the use of classified intelligence as a basis to justify the denial or delay of habeas corpus proceedings
motions.infoslide: Habeas corpus proceedings refers to proceedings where a court examines the legality of someone's detention. Primarily, a court order compels the custodian of a detained person to bring them before the court to determine if the detention is lawful.

motions.motion: THO litigation finance in class action lawsuits
motions.infoslide: Litigation finance refers to the practice of outside investors funding legal claims in exchange for a share of the proceeds if the case is successful. These funders cover legal costs such as attorney fees, expert witnesses, discovery, etc. Typical funders include hedge funds, private equity firms, and specialized litigation finance companies (e.g., Burford Capital, Omni Bridgeway).

motions.motion: THP a single-league playoff system over the East-West conference model in the NBA
motions.infoslide: In the current NBA format, the NBA is structured into two conferences — the Eastern Conference (e.g. Boston Celtics, Philadelphia 76ers, New York Knicks, etc.) and the Western Conference (e.g. Los Angeles Lakers, Golden State Warriors, San Antonio Spurs, etc.) — with 15 teams in each, primarily organized based on geographic location. In the regular season, teams play 82 games where they face teams from the other conference two times and teams from the same conference three or four times. At the end of the season, teams are seeded based on wins in relation to teams within their respective conference. The top teams then proceed to the play-offs. In the current playoff system, teams are seeded within their respective conferences based on regular season performance with the top 6 in each conference qualifying for the playoffs and the 7th-10th teams in each conference entering the Play-In Tournament where teams compete in a single elimination format to determine the lowest playoff seeds at each side of the bracket. In the current format, teams from the East and West can only face each other in the NBA Finals of the play-offs. In a single-league playoff system, the teams are seeded regardless of conference affiliation. Under this model, teams are seeded 1 through 16 based on the total number of wins (e.g., 1 vs. 16, 2 vs. 15, etc.) with the top 12 teams across the league qualifying for the playoffs, and the 13th-20th teams competing in the Play-In tournament. This format abandons the East West conference structure in the play-offs.

motions.motion: THP a world where only one dominant boxing promotion company exists
motions.infoslide: Several boxing associations—such as the WBA, WBC, and WBO—rank fighters and award titles. In boxing, fighters sign with a promoter who helps organize fights for them. Promoters are prohibited from creating their own rankings or titles. The industry includes many promotional companies, such as Golden Boy, Mayweather Promotions, Top Rank, and etc. Organizing fights between fighters usually involves negotiations among different promoters.

motions.motion: THBT Pakistan should offer the Baloch insurgents full amnesty in exchange for permanently surrendering arms
motions.infoslide: The Baloch movement is a nationalist movement seeking greater autonomy and independence for Balochistan, mainly in Pakistan. It stems from grievances over political marginalization, military repression, and control of natural resources. It is split between groups like the Balochistan Liberation Army (BLA) and Balochistan Liberation Front (BLF), which use violence to seek independence, and parties like the BNP (Balochistan National Party), which pursue autonomy through elections and legal means. The BLA has historically resorted to destroying infrastructure, targeting security personnel, and sabotaging foreign investments. Most recently, in March 2025, they hijacked a passenger train, killed five officers, and injured many others.

motions.motion: THBT Dissanayake's Government should return back land previously acquired by the Sri Lankan Military after the 2009 liberation war back to the native Tamils (Areas within the Jaffna Peninsula, Trincomalee, Sampur, etc.)
motions.infoslide: After the Civil War ended in 2009, the Sri Lankan Military acquired vast swathes of land owned by the native minority Tamil population (around 30,000 acres) in the Northern and Eastern Provinces of Sri Lanka. Anura Dissanayake, the newly elected President of Sri Lanka promised to return back land to the Tamils in his electoral campaign. Military bases like Palaly and Kankesanthurai are located in Jaffna, while Trincomalee and Sampur also host major military installations and are declared high-security zones.

motions.motion: THP the sequencing-first model as opposed to the elections-first model of post colonial democratisation in South Asia
motions.infoslide: The sequencing-first model of democratization refers to an approach where liberal-democratic institutions are developed by elites who had significant influence during the colonial era before electoral competition is introduced in nascent political systems. In contrast, the elections-first model prioritizes the rapid introduction of multi-party elections even if the necessary institutions to support fair and effective governance are underdeveloped.

motions.motion: THO all scientific research efforts to genetically engineer endangered species
motions.infoslide: Genetic engineering is the process of directly manipulating an organism's genetic material (DNA) to alter its traits. This involves introducing, removing, or modifying specific genes to achieve a desired outcome.

motions.motion: TH, as the vegan movement, W reject partnerships with lab-grown meat companies in favor of prioritizing investments in whole-food plant-based systems
motions.infoslide: Lab-grown meat companies refer to firms that produce animal meat by cultivating animal cells in bioreactors. Examples include Upside Foods, Mosa Meat, etc. On the other hand, whole-food plant-based (WFPB) systems focus on natural, minimally processed plant foods like vegetables, legumes, grains, nuts, and seeds.

motions.motion: THBT the human obligation to alleviate suffering extends equally to wild animals as it does to pets

THW criminalise all cultural practices that obligate women to renounce their inheritance rights (e.g. giving family wealth to the eldest son in some rich European families, taking land from widows in some African communities, Muslim women accepting less inheritance than men, etc.)
THO the portrayal of abortion as 'without consequence' within feminist spaces
THBT the Japanese feminist movement should support the criminalization of Enjo kōsai
motions.infoslide: Enjo kōsai is a type of transactional relationship where older men give money and/or luxury gifts to attractive young women in exchange for sexual favors in Japan. While Enjo kōsai is not explicitly illegal, Japanese laws prohibit paying a person under 18 for obscene acts and juvenile delinquency.

motions.motion: THO the European Union's recent anti-trust crackdown on the US Big Tech Giants
motions.infoslide: In March 2025, the European Union intensified its antitrust actions against major U.S. tech giants (eg. Apple, Meta, etc.) for alleged violations of the Digital Markets Act. The DMA prohibits self-preferencing and requires interoperability. The companies face fines of up to 10% of their global revenue (20% for repeated violations) along with multiple lawsuits.

motions.motion: THBT all private spaceflight companies (SpaceX, Blue Origin, etc) should prioritize consolidating existing exploration efforts on celestial bodies that have already been explored (Moon, Mars, etc.), as opposed to focusing on unexplored celestial bodies (moons of Jupiter and Saturn, etc.)
THBT Indonesia should make foreign access to its nickel and rare earth minerals conditional on joint development of green technologies, rather than industrial park based capital-only investments
motions.infoslide: The industrial park model for capital-only investments typically refers to a state-coordinated strategy where infrastructure, land, and basic utilities are concentrated within a designated industrial complex - often developed with government or public financing.

motions.motion: THS parents intervening in subtle social conflicts among children in primary school
motions.infoslide: Subtle social conflicts refer to non-physical, emotionally ambiguous interactions that involve exclusion, manipulation, or social imbalance rather than overt aggression. Some examples of such conflict include exclusion-based play, mocking/teasing masked as jokes, ignoring one child inside a group, etc.

motions.motion: In multi-cultural societies, THBT first-generation immigrant parents should instill home country values in their children, even if they contradict liberal norms (e.g., not questioning elders authority)
THP a world where time-bound family formations (e.g., 25-year co-parenting contracts, renewable spousal units, etc.) is the norm
THBT it is in the interest of South Africa to rescind the Expropriation Act
motions.infoslide: South Africa’s latest Expropriation Bill introduces cases where private land can be expropriated by the government without compensation, aiming to address land reform. In response, President Donald Trump signed an executive order to halt all future aid to South Africa until a complete investigation on the issue is completed.

motions.motion: THO the medicalization of gender dysphoria among the queer community
motions.infoslide: Medicalization is the process of defining a phenomenon through medical language in order to categorize it as one that can be treated by medical services (eg. gender affirming services).

motions.motion: THBT queer advocacy groups should prioritize empowering individuals to pursue non-state-dependent relationships (e.g. chosen families, live-in relationships, co parenting, etc.) over campaigning for state-recognized unions (e.g. marriage, adoption, etc.)

TH, as the South Korean LGBTQIA+ movement, W significantly prioritize provinces outside of Seoul in their advocacy (ie. organizing larger pride parades in provinces, financially supporting provincial queer organizations, etc.)
THBT Egypt should accept all refugees fleeing from Gaza
motions.infoslide: In early 2025, President Donald Trump proposed a plan to relocate Palestinians from the Gaza Strip to Egypt, threatening it with scaling back financial assistance from the USA if it refuses to accept the refugees. Egypt has remained cautious about accepting, while continuing to provide humanitarian aid.

motions.motion: THBT the TPLF should have actively attempted to establish internal power sharing within its members before signing the Pretoria Agreement
motions.infoslide: The Tigray People’s Liberation Front (TPLF), the main force in Tigray’s war against Ethiopia’s federal government (2020–2022), signed the Pretoria Agreement in 2022 to end the conflict. At the time, it had a centralized leadership with limited internal power-sharing. After the agreement, it began to fragment with new parties and factions (e.g., Tigray Independence Party, Salsay Weyane Tigray, etc.) emerging in Tigray, some calling for Tigrayan independence and others pushing for different styles of governance.

motions.motion: THO the Grab-GoTo Merger.
motions.infoslide: As of March 20, 2025, Grab Holdings Ltd., a leading Southeast Asian ride- hailing and delivery company headquartered in Singapore, is actively pursuing the acquisition of Indonesian tech conglomerate GoTo Group, which is a dominant player in Indonesia's digital economy, providing services such as ride-hailing and e-commerce.

motions.motion: THW require the GPIF to significantly increase investments in domestic private equity and venture capital funds, even at the expense of reducing investments in its offshore assets
motions.infoslide: The Government Pension Investment Fund (GPIF) is Japan’s primary public pension fund and the largest in the world, managing over ¥220 trillion (approximately USD $1.5 trillion). As of recent years, roughly 50% of GPIF’s portfolio is invested in foreign assets—split evenly between foreign equities and foreign bonds. These include major holdings primarily in the U.S. stock indexes (such as the S&P 500) and debt instruments like the U.S. Treasury securities.

motions.motion: THS the consolidation of fragmented agricultural land holdings through state-directed agrarian restructuring in the Mekong Delta
motions.infoslide: The Mekong Delta—which produces over half of Vietnam’s rice and a significant share of its aquaculture exports—has historically been highly fragmented, with most farms averaging less than 0.5 hectares. A significant proportion of land holdings are held by smallholder households from ethnic minority communities, such as the Khmer and Hoa people, who occupy land without proper documentation and rely on informal recognition by local authorities or customary claims. Typically, these households cultivate various crops (e.g., rice, corn, tapioca, etc.) on their land. The region is experiencing rapid climate change and natural calamities. Recently, the Vietnamese government started pursuing state-directed agrarian restructuring, promoting the consolidation of small, scattered plots into larger farms through state-led land auctions and planned rural relocation programs.

motions.motion: THP a world where the two-kingdoms doctrine was the dominant Christian worldview
motions.infoslide: The traditional ontological belief in Christianity is predicated by a hierarchical theory of structure, where authority flowed from the top down, and laws—both moral and civil—were seen as expressions of this divinely ordained structure, with the church playing a central role in interpreting God’s will and enforcing moral order. In contrast, the Two-Kingdoms doctrine holds that there is a separation between the divine and the earthly. In this view, all people and institutions in the earthly realm are equal in nature and authority. Law in the earthly kingdom is judged by human reason and justice rather than divine hierarchy, and no person or institution mediates another’s relationship with God.

motions.motion: THP a world where all major religions did not obligate their followers to go on religious pilgrimages
motions.infoslide: For the purpose of this debate, pilgrimages are physical journeys to sacred religious sites. Examples include the Hajj to Mecca in Islam, the Camino de Santiago in Christianity, and the Kumbh Mela in Hinduism.

motions.motion: TH, as a sectarian minority leader in an authoritarian Islamic state, would actively encourage their followers to conceal their religious identity even when not under duress
motions.infoslide: Sectarian minorities are groups that differ from the dominant sect of a religion in beliefs, practices, or identity. These typically include religious communities such as Shia Muslims in Sunni-majority countries, or other smaller Islamic sects (e.g., Ismailis, Ahmadis, and etc.).

motions.motion: In ethnically-diverse states that have recently decolonised, THBT national militaries should dismantle residual colonial military structures that prioritise selective ethnic recruitment
motions.infoslide: Selective ethnic recruitment refers to colonial-era policies in which certain ethnic groups were deliberately favored for military service based on stereotypical assumptions about their inherent bravery or martial prowess, while other groups were excluded. Popular examples include the British prioritising the Sikhs and the Gurkhas while deprioritising the Bengalis in India, Northerners (e.g., Hausa-Fulani) being prioritised heavily over southern groups in Nigeria, etc.

motions.motion: TH, as a recently installed dictatorship, W not employ the divide-and-rule coup proofing strategy after overthrowing the previous government
motions.infoslide: Dictatorships often employ the divide-and-rule strategy by fragmenting the military’s power to ensure that no single military group has the power to overthrow them. Some popular examples include Saddam Hussein creating the Republican Guard and the Special Republican Guard separate from the main military in Iraq, Muammar Gaddafi weakening the regular army and creating loyalist militias in Libya, etc.

motions.motion: In states facing armed insurgencies, THS the deployment of Private Military and Security Contractors (eg. The Wagner Group, Blackwater, G4S, etc.) at all critical resource extraction sites in exchange for joint control
TH Predicts that Abrahamic religions will be more decentralised over the next 10 years
TH, as a leader of a small gated religious community, would allow closed-community reality TV to cover their community
motions.infoslide: Closed-community reality TV shows follow outsiders or insiders entering closed religious communities (e.g., the Amish community) to show how strict beliefs, rules, and lifestyles shape everyday life.

motions.motion: THBT it is in the interest of Christian churches to prioritise the relational approach over the proclamation approach in evangelism
motions.infoslide: Evangelism is the process through which churches recruits followers. Churches typically adopt one of two approaches when first approaching potential members. The Relational Approach seeks to attract and retain followers primarily through emphasizing the friendships, community, and social support that religious participation can provide. The Proclamation Approach seeks to attract and retain followers primarily through establishing affinity between the individual and the divine. Common ways include proclamation of the gospel (e.g., sharing that Jesus atoned for humanity's sins through His sacrifice), Catechism, etc.

motions.motion: THBT the popularization of women-made literature (e.g., Heated Rivalry, Fifty Shades of Grey) centered around sexually explicit material has done more harm than good to women
For weaker states in asymmetric warfare, TH prefers a strategy of prioritising economic resilience over bolstering military strength
THBT the feminist movement should strongly emphasise the "manadult" framework as a standard for male partners
motions.infoslide: A "manadult" refers to a male partner who actively practices anti-patriarchal maturity. The 'manadult' framework focuses on 5 charateristics: independent deconstruction of patriarchy rather than relying on women to educate him, seeking and accepting feminist feedback, consuming women's media, proactively taking on care labour (e.g., household chores, emotional support), and emotionally diverse friendships.

motions.motion: THBT the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) should lower the threshold for mandatory delisting
motions.infoslide: In China, publicly traded companies may face mandatory delisting for reasons including major financial fraud, sustained losses, insolvency, or low trading volume. Firms identified to have serious financial or compliance risks are often flagged as risky for several years before possible delisting. China’s largest equity market historically delisted less than 0.3% of its companies per year. For the purposes of this debate, “lowering the threshold for mandatory delisting” means expanding the circumstances under which companies are required to leave public exchanges and allowing regulators to delist companies more readily.

motions.motion: THBT the EU should significantly relax labor regulations (e.g., minimum wage, overtime regulations, severance pay, employee councils’ veto power over firing decisions)
THO Project Stargate
motions.infoslide: Project Stargate is an American artificial intelligence infrastructure joint venture created by OpenAI, SoftBank, Oracle, and investment firm MGX, with technology partners including Microsoft, Nvidia, and Arm, announced at the White House on January 21, 2025, by President Trump. The venture plans to invest up to US$500 billion in AI data centers and related infrastructure in the United States by 2029, beginning with an initial US$100 billion phase.

motions.motion: THR the rise of celebrity politicians (e.g. Imran Khan, Shakib Al Hasan, Thelapathy Vijay) in South Asia.
THP a world where key political and social activists (e.g: Malala) are viewed as ordinary rather than exceptional
TH, as the anti-AI movement, would prioritize existential risks over near-term harms in its messaging

THBT social movements should engage in restructuring (e.g. limiting expected workloads, rotating leadership and responsibilities, reducing meeting intensity or frequency) in response to activist burnout
THBT college accreditation should focus on output (e.g., learning assessment, graduation rate, earnings) rather than input (e.g., curriculum structure, faculty qualification, governance)
motions.infoslide: Accreditation is the review system that determines whether colleges and programs meet acceptable standards. Receiving accredited status is usually necessary for access to government funding or student loans.

motions.motion: TH prefers the grade-specialization model over the cohort-continuity model in secondary education
motions.infoslide: In the grade-specialization model, teachers repeatedly teach the same grade level each year (e.g., always teaching 10th grade students), but teach different cohorts over time. In the cohort-continuity model, teachers move up through school with the same group of students over multiple years (e.g., teaching the same class from 10th to 12th grade).

motions.motion: THBT China should adopt a fully standardized national Gaokao system
motions.infoslide: The Gaokao, China’s national university entrance examination, is currently administered with substantial regional variation. Students generally take the examination in their home province, and universities allocate admission quotas for each different region. Different regions differ in subjects, difficulty, scoring systems, and examination formats. Students are primarily evaluated relative to others within their own provincial system.

motions.motion: THR the use of WLFI as the WLF's primary form of collateral
motions.infoslide: World Liberty Financial (WLF) is a decentralized finance, or DeFi, project that provides crypto-based financial services such as lending, borrowing, and stablecoin-related products. WLFI is WLF’s own governance token, which is tied to participation in the ecosystem and allows holders to vote on certain decisions within the platform. At the same time, WLFI is also a financial asset that has increasingly been used as collateral in WLF-linked lending, borrowing, and liquidity arrangements within its DeFi ecosystem.

motions.motion: THO the tokenization of financial markets
motions.infoslide: The tokenization of financial markets refers to the use of digital tokens—recorded on blockchain networks—to represent ownership of financial assets such as equity, bonds, or real estate. Financial institutions such as JPMorgan Chase have developed token-based payment and settlement systems.

motions.motion: THBT cryptocurrency markets should be regulated under a commodities-market model rather than an investor-protection model
motions.infoslide: U.S. crypto regulation is currently split between the Securities Exchange Commission (SEC) and Commodities Future Trading Commission (CFTC), with many digital assets classified case by case through enforcement, guidance, and litigation. A commodities-market model, enforced by the CFTC, focuses on exchange oversight, custody, market integrity, and anti-manipulation rules (e.g. in gold or oil markets). On the other hand, an investor-protection model, enforced by the SEC, focuses on issuer disclosures, registration, and safeguards for buyers investing in projects that promise returns (e.g. in stock markets).

motions.motion: In multi-ethnic post-conflict states, TH prefers the implementation of a Presidential form of government over a Parliamentary form of government
motions.infoslide: In a Presidential system, the President is directly elected by the people, and acts as both Head of State and Head of Government. The executive branch is completely separate from the legislature. In a Parliamentary system, the Prime Minister, who is the Head of Government, is not directly elected by the public but is the leader of the majority party or coalition in Parliament.

motions.motion: THBT Singapore should adopt the Kishore approach to foreign policy as opposed to the Bilahari approach
motions.infoslide: Kishore Mahbubani and Bilahari Kausikan are two prominent Singaporean foreign policy thinkers. Kishore argues small states must always be conscious of their limitations, exercising caution and restraint in public positions so as not to unnecessarily antagonise great powers. Bilahari advocates for small states to stand firm on sovereignity and international law, fiercely safeguarding national interests regardless of how great powers may feel.

motions.motion: THBT it is in ASEAN's interest to recognize the elections in Myanmar
TH Regrets Cambodia's invoking of the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS) compulsory conciliation mechanism
motions.infoslide: The Cambodia–Thailand maritime dispute centres on an Overlapping Claims Area (OCA) in the Gulf of Thailand. In 2001, the two countries signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU-44) to provide a framework for resolving the boundary and jointly developing the energy resources. However, in May 2026, Thailand's government unilaterally terminated MOU-44. On June 2, 2026, Prime Minister Hun Manet formally notified both Thailand and the UN Secretary-General that Cambodia was invoking compulsory conciliation under UNCLOS Annex V, Section 2. This mechanism creates a conciliation commission to hear both sides, examine the facts and legal arguments, and issue recommendations.

motions.motion: THP a world where Sulu joined the BARMM
motions.infoslide: The Bangsomoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) is a special autonomous region in the southern Philippines created to give Muslim-majority areas stronger self-government. Sulu is an island province with a Muslim- majority population. In the 2019 plebiscite on the law creating BARMM, 54% of Sulu's population voted against the law. In 2024, the Supreme Court ruled that Sulu’s “No” vote had to be respected, so the province was removed from BARMM and placed under Region IX (Zamboanga Peninsula), with a dominantly Christian-majority population. Zamboanga Peninsula is one of the nearest regular administrative regions to Sulu, with Zamboanga City serving as a major transport, trade, port, and service hub for the Sulu Archipelago.

motions.motion: TH Predicts that the global population living in poverty* will increase, rather than decrease, in the next 10 years *living on less than USD4.20 per day adjusted for inflation and purchasing power
THR the US Shale Boom
motions.infoslide: The US shale boom refers to the massive surge in oil and natural gas production in the United States, driven by advances in horizontal drilling and hydraulic fracturing since 2010.

motions.motion: TH Predicts that international relations will become more liberalist in the next 25 years
motions.infoslide: Liberalism in international relations (IR) is a theoretical perspective emphasizing that states can achieve cooperation, mutual benefits, and peace through international institutions, and economic interdependence.

motions.motion: TH, as a young adult, would prefer to build our life with a partner who works in the same industry rather than someone with a significantly different career
In Asian countries with low birthrates, THS government-run dating services (e.g., FirstDate in Singapore)
THS the trend of roster dating
motions.infoslide: Roster dating is the modern practice of casually dating or talking to multiple people simultaneously to keep options open, rather than focusing on one person.

motions.motion: THW abolish mega auctions in the IPL
motions.infoslide: The Indian Premier League (IPL) is a professional men's cricket league. In this league, teams acquire players mainly through an annual auction. In an annual auction, players are listed with a base price, and teams bid for them, with the highest bidder securing the player's services. Franchises must operate within a salary cap and squad size limits. A Mega Auction is a special form of the annual auction held every 3-4 years. It serves as a complete squad reset where teams can retain only a small core of 4-6 players. In contrast, the regular annual auction does not restrict teams from retaining as many players.

motions.motion: TH, as a national team coach, would prioritize intrinsic over extrinsic motivation
motions.infoslide: Coaching models prioritizing extrinsic motivation are models which reward or punish athletes based on their performance. Coaching models which prioritize intrinsic motivation are models which focus on increasing inherent satisfaction when engaging in the sport, e.g. enjoying the sport, serving the team.

motions.motion: TH, as a rising player in esports, would prefer to be signed as a support main over a duelist main
motions.infoslide: In hero-shooter games like Valorant and Marvel Rivals, team composition depends on a mixture of roles and characters. A support main usually helps the team through utility like flashes, recon, smokes, healing, site control, or defensive setups rather than being the primary player expected to kill. A Duelist, by contrast, is an aggressive role built to take first contact, enter contested areas, create space, and win fights. Duelists are expected to seek early engagements and get high-value kills, which requires quick reflexes and reaction times.

motions.motion: THBT Bangladesh should grant more autonomy to its local governments
THS Pakistan's aggressive security measures against cross-border militancy from Afghanistan
THO the rise of minmaxxing
motions.infoslide: Minmaxxing means optimizing something by maximizing its strengths and minimizing or ignoring its weaknesses. It’s often used to describe approaches to life, work, fitness, dating, or productivity.

motions.motion: THR the stigmatization against being chronically online
THBT the BJP's win is in interest of the citizens of West Bengal
motions.infoslide: For the past 15 years, West Bengal had been ruled by a populist leader named Mamata Bannerjee from the regional party TMC. Recently, she lost the State elections in a landslide to the BJP.

motions.motion: THR the prevalence of ‘kiasuism’ in Southeast Asia
motions.infoslide: Kiasuism is a cultural phenomenon derived from the Hokkien word "kiasu" (驚輸), which means "afraid to lose." It describes a pervasive social mindset characterised by an intense fear of missing out, losing, or being worse off than others.

motions.motion: THBT it is in the interest of Japan to further invest in arms manufacturing and sales to Southeast Asia
THBT Operation Epic Fury has done more harm than good to the Trump Administration
TH prefers the Loans Programs Office Model to the Tax Credit Model for Clean Energy Financing
motions.infoslide: The government finances clean energy in two main ways. The Loan Programs Office (LPO) directly lends government money to clean energy projects. The tax credit model, most prominently used in the IRA, instead reduces the tax liability of companies that deploy qualifying clean energy assets like solar farms or electric vehicles.

motions.motion: THS the ASEAN Power Grid (APG)
motions.infoslide: The ASEAN Power Grid (APG) is a regional initiative aimed at interconnecting the national power systems of Southeast Asian countries.

motions.motion: In times of energy crisis, THS subsidizing energy consumption rather than reducing energy demand (e.g., extend holidays, enforce fuel rationing)
THBT the feminist movement should advocate for the feminization strategy over the neutralization strategy
motions.infoslide: In many languages, masculine forms are often used when gender is not specified. Two main approaches aim to make language more inclusive. Feminisation makes women more visible by naming women and men equally. For example, instead of saying “the applicant… he,” one might say “the applicant… he or she.” Other examples include using split forms such as “Vegetarier/innen” in German to encompass both “Vegetarier” (the male form) and "Vegetarierinnen" (the female form). Neutralisation reduces the focus on gender by using gender-neutral words and pronouns. For example, Swedish uses “hen” as a neutral alternative to “hon” and “han,” while Norwegian has moved away from some feminine suffixes.

motions.motion: THR the establishment of regional blocs in Africa e.g. COMESA, EAC, SADC, ECOWAS
THR the dominance of the narrative that love is only legitimate if it is long-term.
THBT the rise of public intellectuals has done more harm than good.
motions.infoslide: The term 'public intellectual' describes the intellectual participating in the public affairs discourse of society, in addition to an academic career.

motions.motion: THP a cashless world
THS the decision by the Government of Kenya to lift the ban on GMOs.
motions.infoslide: In October 2022, the Government of Kenya revoked a long-standing ban on the importation of Genetically Modified Crops (GMOs).

motions.motion: THP afterlife A over Afterlife B
motions.infoslide: In afterlife A, after a life well lived, the 'ethereal soul' is separated from the 'physical body' and joins an eternal, blissful community of souls and 'God' (no physical body). In afterlife B, after a life well lived, the 'ethereal soul' is separated from the 'physical body' and leads a next life, in a new body, in a new world with rewards from the previous life carried into the new world. After the life in the new world ends, the 'ethereal soul' leaves the physical body and leads into another subsequent world under the same conditions. In this version of the afterlife, the soul is on an endless journey of moving from one world to another carrying accumulations of moral rewards from previous lives into every new life. In both instances, people have the same knowledge about the afterlife as in the status quo.

motions.motion: THP a world with the BCS
motions.infoslide: A Behaviour Credit System (BCS) is a worldwide integrated technology that allow persons to vote on the social standing of anyone living in that country. The BCS can be accessed through any technological device whereby upon interacting with you, people can rate you on a numerical scale on their device based on their personal and discretionary feelings about you (not on legal grounds). In this system, businesses independently set minimum positive point and maximum negative point limits as requirements before they render services to you and by law, your positive or negative points tally has a bearing on the extent of punishment you get when you are tried for a crime.

motions.motion: Without foresight, THW rather be a late bloomer than a child prodigy
THBT globalization has failed
THS the creation and use of municipal bonds within developing countries in Africa.
motions.infoslide: Municipal bonds (or “munis” for short) are debt securities issued by districts, cities, counties and other governmental entities to fund day-to-day obligations and to finance capital projects.

motions.motion: THW establish universal jurisdiction for biodiversity.
THS the continued existence of consumer company monopolies in Africa.
THBT that the AU should recognise the statehood of Somaliland.
motions.infoslide: Somaliland is an autonomous region in northern Somalia, which broke away and declared independence from Somalia in 1991. No foreign power recognises Somaliland's sovereignty.

motions.motion: THS the gig economy in developing Africa countries
motions.infoslide: "The gig economy" is a term used to describe a labor market where freelance or short-term contracts are more common than permanent positions. Independent contractors are those who work in the gig economy. They perform services for another party in accordance with the conditions mentioned in a verbal or written agreement. In contrast to an employee, an independent contractor works for an employer on an as-needed basis rather than on a regular basis. Since they typically receive freelance compensation, independent contractors are considered "self employed."

motions.motion: THBT the criteria for determining the G.O.A.T in team sports, such as football and basketball, should prioritise individual achievements and impact over team success and championships.
THR the glamorisation of being a savage in pop culture
THW Hold accountable all existing African kingdoms that took part in the Trans-Atlantic slave trade
THW prohibit the media from reporting on the mental illness of those accused of crimes
THR eco-terrorism by citizens in the Niger Delta
motions.infoslide: Eco-terrorism refers to the use or threat of violence against property or people, committed in order to forward environmental causes or for environmental-political reasons.

motions.motion: THW use Social Impact Bonds for charitable programmes as opposed to direct government funding
motions.infoslide: A "Social Impact Bond" is a funding model where governments arrange for private investors to fund a service provider to solve a specified social problem within a pre-alloted time frame determined by the government. The government only repays the investors if the project is 'successful', as set out in the original contract. If successful, the government repays the investors’ upfront investment plus an additional premium.

motions.motion: THS The Rise of Product Placement in the Production of Movies
motions.infoslide: Product placement has long been a feature of Hollywood. Seeking a boost in brand recognition and association with cool characters, alcohol and car companies, especially, have for decades paid or engaged in a kind of quid pro quo to get their products into films. The first documented example was in 1896, when the Lumière brothers, often credited as the earliest filmmakers, agreed to feature soap in their film “Washing Day in Switzerland.” But the rise of streaming has led to an explosion in product placement. Product placement is now a $23 billion industry, up by an estimated 14 percent since 2020.

motions.motion: THW ban the use of gene editing technology for the creation of genetically enhanced children.
THR the "Kill Them With Kindness" narrative.
motions.infoslide: "Kill them with kindness" is an idiom used by Shakespeare that means to overwhelm someone with over-indulgence. It can mean that in itself but it can also imply that someone can be harmed, inconvenienced, or bothered by being treated with excessive favour or kindness.

motions.motion: THBT Economically unstable countries should leverage foreign insurrections that boost it's economy e.g Turkey leveraging power over the Gulf Nations via it's ties to the Muslim Brotherhood, the US leveraging power over the middle east via its ocuupation of Afghanistan or the Taliban leveraging Uighurs in China for legitimacy.
As the Pevensie siblings, THW never leave Narnia.
motions.infoslide: During World War II, the Pevensie children, Peter, Susan, Edmund and Lucy, are evacuated from a London to live in a stranger’s country house. While playing hide and side, they discover a wardrobe and enters the fantasy world of Narnia. After defeating the White Witch and saving Narnia, the siblings are crowned Kings and Queens of Narnia. Fifteen years later, the Pevensies, now young adults, and discover the wardrobe they entered Narnia from. Presume for the purposes of this debate that the Pevensies are aware that, should they depart Narnia, they may never return, and will return to their residence in their former child forms (but with memories intact) seconds after they left.

motions.motion: Champagne socialism refers to a form of socialism where a group of rich / upper class individuals advocate for a fair society where everyone has equal rights and the rich people help the poor.
motions.infoslide: THW promote champagne socialism in Africa.

motions.motion: This House as Ethan would continue the romantic relationship with Mia.
motions.infoslide: Ethan is deeply in love with Mia, and their connection feels genuine and profound. They have shared countless moments, dreams, and experiences together, building a relationship based on trust and affection. However, Ethan’s world is shaken when he discovers that Mia is not a naturally born person, but a genetically engineered clone. Despite Mia’s assurances that her love for him is real and that she has developed her own identity, Ethan must grapple with whether the bond they’ve formed is strong enough to overcome the fact that she was created, not born, and whether it changes the authenticity of their love

motions.motion: This House supports the formation of an African Super League (ASL)
motions.infoslide: The African Super League (ASL)would be a proposed elite football competition featuring top clubs from across the continent. It aims to bring together the best teams in Africa to compete in a league. Teams participating in the ASL would not play in their local country leagues.

motions.motion: TH as a tech university graduate would rather primarily focus their efforts on becoming an entrepreneur rather than going for a corporate job
This House Regrets IMF structural adjustment programs in African nations.
motions.infoslide: Structural Adjustment Policies (SAPs) are economic reforms implemented by countries as a condition for receiving financial assistance from international institutions like the IMF and World Bank. These policies typically include reducing government spending, privatizing state-owned enterprises, liberalizing trade, devaluing currency, and encouraging foreign investment.

motions.motion: This House Believes that secession movements should be allowed to vote for independence without central government approval
This House Would prioritize smaller local, unconditional aid over larger foreign, conditional aid for NGOs in developing countries
motions.infoslide: A small NGO in a developing country has two funding options: a large foreign donor offering substantial financial support with strict conditions, or a local foundation providing smaller, unrestricted aid. The foreign aid comes with the promise of scaling operations but requires the NGO to implement programs that may not fully align with the local community's cultural needs, as the donor has set specific goals and oversight protocols. While the funding would allow the NGO to reach a broader audience, it would compromise its ability to independently design programs suited to the local context. On the other hand, the local aid comes with no conditions, giving the NGO complete freedom to tailor its programs to the community’s needs. However, the local funding is limited, which means the NGO can’t expand its reach as significantly.

motions.motion: THBT African billionaires have a moral obligation to invest a significant portion of their wealth to philanthropic efforts.
This House Believes that Youth Movements in Kenya should remain grassroots movements without leaders rather than evolving into political movements with leaders.
motions.infoslide: In Kenya, youth protests have been a significant part of the political landscape in 2024, especially in the context of economic challenges, rising taxes, and the demand for systemic reforms. Youth across the country have mobilized to demand change on issues such as unemployment, education, and economic empowerment

motions.motion: THW actively prioritize Universal Basic Income (UBI) over Universal Basic Services.
motions.infoslide: Universal Basic Services (UBS) is a system that guarantees free or affordable access to essential public services, such as healthcare, education, housing, and transportation, aiming to meet basic human needs and promote social equity whereas a Universal Basic Income (UBI) is an unconditional cash payment given monthly to all citizens or residents, regardless of their employment status, to ensure a basic standard of living and reduce poverty. For the purposes of this debate, the amount given to a citizen monthly under a UBI program is proportional to the amount the government would have spent to subsidise the citizen across the various UBS programs.

motions.motion: Assuming Feasibility, This House Believes that Artificial Intelligence (AI) should be allowed to make life-and-death decisions in healthcare, including triage and end-of-life care, rather than leaving such decisions to human doctors and families.

This House opposes the practice of companies patenting or trademarking cultural elements or phrases that do not originate from their country of domicile eg Disney's Trademarking of "Hakuna Matata", The Patent of Basmati Rice by a Western Company or The Trademark of Mexican Tequila by Non-Mexican Companies
This House opposes Sahel states forming The Alliance of Sahel States
motions.infoslide: The Sahel is a region spanning Africa, including countries such as Mali, Niger, Burkina Faso, Chad, and Mauritania. This year, Burkina Faso, Mali and Niger had exited ECOWOS to form the Alliance of Sahel States.

motions.motion: THR the decline of Ubuntu in African Communities.
motions.infoslide: Ubuntu is a Nguni Bantu term from Southern Africa, meaning "humanity" or "I am because we are." It emphasizes the interconnectedness of people and the importance of community, compassion, and mutual support

motions.motion: THR the societal expectation to "leave a legacy."
THP a World In Which the African Nations Championship (CHAN) was replaced with a quota system for domestic players at the Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON)
motions.infoslide: CHAN is the biennial African Nations Championship, a football tournament organized by the Confederation of African Football (CAF) exclusively for players who play in their national domestic leagues.

motions.motion: THR the concept of "South African Exceptionalism"
⁠THO the rise of retail/crypto investing in Africa
motions.infoslide: Retail/Crypto investors are non-professional individual investors who buy and sell financial assets (e.g stocks, bonds, commodities,cryptocurrency etc.). In Africa, applications like Valr, Luno, Binance and Chaka have sparked significant interest in retail trading while several major African stock exchanges like the Egyptian Exchange (EGX) have released mobile apps in 2025.

motions.motion: THBT African states should form a collective bargaining cartel for critical minerals (e.g., cobalt, lithium).
THBT the LGBTQ movement should adopt a gender-abolitionist approach as opposed to a gender-pluralist one.
motions.infoslide: A gender abolitionist approach is one that seeks to eliminate the social, legal, and cultural significance of gender. A gender pluralist approach seeks to recognize and protect a multitude of gender identities

motions.motion: THR the dominance of movies based on already existing Intellectual property (IP) in contemporary cinema
THS Saudi Arabia joining the Abraham Accords on the condition of a clear commitment towards a two-state solution from Israel.
motions.infoslide: The Abraham Accords are a series of diplomatic agreements brokered by the USA between Israel and several Arab nations, including the United Arab Emirates, Bahrain, Morocco, and Sudan. The accords establish diplomatic relations and promote collaboration across multiple sectors such as trade, education, and technology.

motions.motion: THBT fiscal stimulus is more effective than monetary stimulus in a recession.
motions.infoslide: Fiscal stimulus is when a Government boosts demand by increasing spending or cutting taxes. Monetary stimulus is when the Central bank boosts demand by lowering interest rates or expanding the money supply.

motions.motion: TH as an agnostic person would adopt Pascal’s wager
motions.infoslide: Pascal’s Wager argues that when we cannot be certain about God’s existence, belief is the safer choice. Believing in the Christian God as the one and true only God and being right after living a life of adopting Christian values and practices may lead to eternal salvation (an infinite reward), while not believing and being wrong while pursuing other ‘worldly’ values and practices may lead to eternal damnation (an infinite loss).

motions.motion: THBT research organisations in the Global South should prioritise South-to-South collaborations instead of partnering with institutions in the Global North
THBT African states should withdraw recognition of the ICJ’s compulsory jurisdiction in response to its failure to uphold justice in Sudan.
motions.infoslide: On March 6, 2025, Sudan filed an application to initiate a case at the International Court of Justice (ICJ) against the United Arab Emirates (UAE) for violations of the Genocide Convention. It alleges that the UAE, “through its direction and provision of extensive financial, political and military support for the rebel RSF (Rapid Support Forces) militia,” is complicit in genocide. In May 2025, the International Court of Justice (ICJ) said that it “manifestly lacked” the authority to continue the proceedings and threw out the case because the UAE, upon ratification of the Convention, made a reservation to Article 9, the compromissory clause that grants the Court jurisdiction over disputes related to the Convention.

motions.motion: TH, as an average 20-year-old African person, would enter the Waka-Trix.
motions.infoslide: The Waka-Trix (Wakanda Matrix) is a fully immersive digital world presenting a plausible alternate Africa and global system in which colonialism and its direct consequences never occurred. This world is still subject to natural, political, and social imperfection. Upon entry to the Waka-Trix, your physical body dies, and all contact with the real world ceases. You enter the Waka-Trix as a newborn baby, with no memories or continued consciousness from the real world.

motions.motion: ECCQ las megaciudades latinoamericanas (Buenos Aires, Rio de Janeiro, etc.) deberían reestructurarse hacia el 'urbanismo de los 15 minutos’.
motions.infoslide: El **'urbanismo de los 15 minutos'** es una estrategia de planeación territorial que busca que las ciudadanas puedan acceder a servicios en un radio aproximado de 15 minutos caminando o en bicicleta desde su casa. Se busca organizar la ciudad en pequeños núcleos, y generar instalaciones en los ámbitos de vivienda, trabajo, comercio, sanidad, educación y entretenimiento. Los ejemplos más prominentes de este modelo son los planes de “Paris en Commun”, el "Great City Plan" de Shanghai y el "Plan Melbourne". A efectos de este debate, una megaciudad es aquella con un mínimo de 10 millones de habitantes y una alta densidad demográfica.

motions.motion: EC investigaría desde el enfoque de las causas en lugar de las consecuencias.
motions.infoslide: Eres una investigadora de ciencias sociales experimentadas. Estás comprometida con los derechos sociales y has realizado múltiples investigaciones de campo. Para efectos de este debate tienes que escoger entre dos enfoques metodológicos para tu investigación sobre el auge de movimientos de ultraderecha. El **enfoque de causas** implica habilitar espacios de diálogo con comunidades de derechas y las personas que las conforman. Así, tu proyecto de investigación podría incluir entrevistas y reuniones cercanas con personas conservadoras para comprender su visión desde la empatía. El **enfoque de las consecuencias** implica habilitar espacios de diálogo con las comunidades afectadas por estos movimientos. Así, tu proyecto de investigación podría incluir entrevistas grupales con personas migrantes para comprender sus vivencias.

motions.motion: EC dividiría Penguin Random House en sellos editoriales.
motions.infoslide: Penguin Random House (RRH) es la editorial más grande del mundo, con presencia en más de 20 países, publicaciones en más de 30 idiomas y un posicionamiento significativo en el mercado global del libro impreso y digital. En el ámbito hispanohablante, posee los derechos de 42 sellos editoriales, entre los que destacan Alfaguara, Debolsillo, Debate y Taurus. En EEUU sus títulos ocuparon el 36.7% de los puestos en la lista de bestsellers de tapa dura en 2023 y en 2024 alcanzó una cuota de mercado del 25%.

motions.motion: EC prefiere un mundo donde la concepción dominante sobre el derecho sea la principialista sobre la contextual.
motions.infoslide: Para fines de este debate existen 2 concepciones sobre el derecho. La **concepción principialista** entiende el derecho como un conjunto de normas que son válidas porque respetan directrices éticas universales como la igualdad o la dignidad. Donde esta concepción, se asume que las juezas interpretan los textos legales de una forma lógica, sin dejarse guiar por su opinión personal. Por ejemplo, se entiende que la eutanasia es un derecho porque coincide con el principio de libertad personal y vida digna. La **concepción contextual** entiende el derecho como el conjunto de decisiones judiciales que son válidas por la única razón de que han sido adoptadas por una jueza. Desde esta concepción se asume que las juezas deciden sus fallos guiadas por sus valores e ideologías. Por ejemplo, se entiende que la eutanasia es un derecho, en algunos Estados, porque juezas liberales tomaron esa decisión.

motions.motion: EC implementaría la pedagogía post radical en el sistema educativo tradicional.
motions.infoslide: La **Pedagogía Post Radical** es una propuesta crítica basada en la resistencia activa a las prioridades normativas del sistema educativo tradicional. Algunas de las propuestas por las que aboga son: \- Que las educadoras usen prácticas artísticas para enseñar todas las asignaturas (p.e escrita creativa para enseñar biología, obras de teatro para enseñar historia, o dibujo artístico para enseñar matemáticas). \- Que el diseño de planes educativos despriorice resultados de aprendizaje basado en respuestas cerradas y estandarizadas en favor de resultados de aprendizajes experienciales y críticos. \- Que las instituciones de educación primaria y secundaria ganen independencia y competencias para adaptar el curriculum a su contexto, más alla de lo estipulado a nivel estatal.

motions.motion: EC se opone a la Línea de Crédito Flexible del FMI
motions.infoslide: La Línea de Crédito Flexible (LCF) es un instrumento creado por el Fondo Monetario Internacional (FMI) para proveer financiamiento con condicionamientos menos rígidos a países con indicadores económicos estables e institucionales fuertes. Esta línea tiene dos características básicas que la diferencian de otras facilidades del FMI: 1\. Su propósito es atender las afectaciones graves causadas por factores excepcionales. 2\. Es un instrumento no condicional, donde se puede acceder a los fondos sin compromisos posteriores de política económica.

motions.motion: ECCQ los partidos políticos de izquierdas deberían priorizar las políticas universalistas por sobre las progresivas.
motions.infoslide: Las políticas universalistas son políticas públicas orientadas a que toda la población pueda beneficiarse por igual, independientemente de su perfil socioeconómico. Algunos ejemplos son la reducción de la jornada laboral o la inclusión de la terapia psicológica en la sanidad pública universal. Las políticas progresivas son políticas públicas dirigidas específicamente a los sectores de menos ingresos o con mayor nivel de vulnerabilidad. Entre ellas se encuentran las subvenciones para personas en riesgo de exclusión social y la construcción de viviendas protegidas para familias de bajos ingresos.

motions.motion: ECCQ República Dominicana debería acelerar sus políticas de transición energética.
EC prefiere la ética zoroastrista en lugar de la ética taoísta
motions.infoslide: La ética zoroastrista de la salvación se centra en el cuidado hacia otras personas. Para ello, invita a realizar realizar acciones desinteresadas que prioricen el bienestar de las demás. La ética taoísta de la liberación se centra en el autocuidado del cuerpo y la mente. Para ello, invita a alejarse del sufrimiento propio mediante la disciplina con una misma.

motions.motion: EC, siendo el movimiento LGBTIAQ+ en los países del norte global, priorizaría un activismo basado en la "denuncia como resistencia" por encima del "placer como resistencia".
motions.infoslide: La denuncia como resistencia es una estrategia política que se centra en visibilizar la discriminación e injusticias que viven las personas LGBTIAQ+, exigiendo una transformación social desde la indignación, el coraje y la memoria de sufrimiento colectivo. Algunos ejemplos son marchas con consignas como “Nos están matando” o documentales como "Paris Is Burning", que denuncian la transfobia sistémica en los medios y la sociedad. El placer como resistencia centra la estrategia política en valorar el goce y el bienestar de las personas LGBTIAQ+ reivindicando que vivir con plenitud es un acto profundamente revolucionario. En lugar de poner el foco en las injusticias, lucha por imaginar futuros desde la alegría, el amor y el cuidado mutuo como motores de cambio. Algunos ejemplos son fiestas queer con espacios para la exploración sexual segura o series como "Heartstopper", que muestran relaciones queer positivas y felices.

motions.motion: EC prefiere que la crítica artística se rija predominantemente por el CAMP en lugar del AURA
motions.infoslide: La crítica artística es aquella que describe, analiza e interpreta diversas manifestaciones artísticas desde esferas académicas y periodísticas. Incluye, entre otras: reseñas, ensayos, perfiles de artistas y tesis. Para fines de este debate, existen dos formas de plantear la crítica artística contemporánea: * La sensibilidad CAMP valora todas las expresiones artísticas por igual. Para lograrlo, utiliza la ironía y señala la ridiculez del arte, mientras que reivindica formas de arte masivo y estéticas populares. A través de esta lente, una crítica CAMP equipararía 'Hamlet' con 'Ru Paul's Drag Race', o telenovelas con películas de culto; valorando los personajes, actuaciones extravagantes y el dramatismo de las tramas de ambas. * La sensibilidad AURA valora ciertas expresiones artísticas como más importantes. Para lograrlo, utiliza el análisis formal para buscar un subtexto profundo en el arte, a la vez que reivindica formas de arte únicas y que presentan una sólida base conceptual. A través de esta lente, una crítica AURA defendería el valor diferencial de una pintura original de Van Gogh en comparación de una impresión postal de la misma, enfatizando el esfuerzo introspectivo específico del proceso de la artista.

motions.motion: ECCQ el Gobierno Federal de Etiopía debería delegar la gestión de los conflictos con milicias étnicas a las policías regionales
motions.infoslide: Etiopía es un Estado Federal compuesto por 9 Estados regionales, cada uno con una etnia mayoritaria diferente. En octubre de 2022, el Gobierno Federal Etíope y el Frente Popular de Liberación de Tigray firmaron el Acuerdo de Paz de Pretoria, poniendo fin a casi dos años de conflicto. Sin embargo, la violencia se ha mantenido entre el Gobierno Federal y milicias étnicas como Fano y el Ejército de Liberación Oromo (OLA), que luchan para que sus Estados regionales tengan mayor autonomía respecto al Estado Federal. Hasta ahora, el Gobierno Federal ha combatido a las milicias de manera centralizada a través del Ejército Federal, que es un cuerpo de defensa multiétnico. Además, los 9 Estados cuentan con policías regionales propias formadas por la etnia mayoritaria de cada Estado.

motions.motion: EC, siendo un unicornio tecnológico en expansión, priorizaría financiarse con prestamistas directos en lugar de empresas de capital de inversión
motions.infoslide: Un unicornio tecnológico es una startup privada de base tecnológica cuya valoración actual es de al menos 1.000 millones de dólares. Algunos ejemplos de unicornios tecnológicos son SpaceX, Canva, ByteDnace y OpenAI. Un prestamista directo (direct lender) es una entidad financiera no bancaria que proporciona financiamiento a empresas de manera directa, sin intermediar con bancos comerciales. Suele tener procesos ágiles y condiciones personalizadas, a cambio de un mayor costo de financiación. Algunos ejemplos de prestamistas directos son Ares Management, Blackstone Credit y Oaktree Capital. Una empresa de capital de inversión gestiona fondos privados e invierte capital propio (equity) en otras empresas para hacerlas crecer. Suelen tomar parte del control de las empresas en que invierten adquiriendo participaciones accionariales.
