THBT ethnic Chinese communities in Southeast Asia should aggressively distance themselves from China (eg. rejecting “finding your roots” trips to China, identifying as culturally distinct to Mainland China, avoiding doing business or study in China despite China's overseas-Chinese-friendly policies)
motions.infoslide: "Ethnic Chinese" persons refer to foreign nationals of ancestral Chinese descent, but whose political identification aligns to the country they are citizens of (e.g., ethnic Chinese groups who are citizens of Malaysia). China has sought to build connections with ethnic Chinese groups outside of China. Examples include inviting ethnic Chinese individuals to China's Two Sessions parliamentary meeting, including ethnic Chinese participants at Chinese business conventions, appealing to young ethnic Chinese inviduals to learn Mandarin, and framing the 2024 Chinese New Year Gala Performance as being for "all Chinese" globally.

motions.motion: THW require private equity firms to assume the entirety of the debt obligations assumed during leveraged buyouts
motions.infoslide: (1) A private equity firm is a type of investment firm open to a select group of investors that acquires companies with the aim of either improving them and selling them at a profit later down the line, or selling the company's assets for more than they bought it for. (2) Most private equity firms acquire companies through Leveraged Buyouts (LBOs) which involves borrowing a large proportion of the money needed to purchase a company and paying it off through the income generated by the company's operations. The debt obligations are typically forced onto the company being acquired, meaning the private equity firm itself is not liable for any potential default and its credit worthiness is unaffected.

motions.motion: THBT the international community's attempts to ostracise Israel have done more harm than good
In countries with competitive national elections, THS the use of city council dismissals
motions.infoslide: City Council Dismissals (CCDs) allow the central government to replace local- government executives (e.g. mayors, councillors, etc.) they deem to be engaging in criminal activity, with experienced non-elected civil servants from a different region for the remainder of the original term.

motions.motion: THO the institutional condemnation of traditional masculinity (e.g. APA officially publishing that tradtitional masculinity is harmful to health, UN declaring traditional masculinity as a leading cause of Gender-Based Violence, etc.)
THS the resurgence of Industrial Policy as a tool for spurring economic growth
motions.infoslide: Industrial Policy is defined as the strategic effort by the state to encourage economic transformation by specifically advantaging what it deems to be high- potential sectors. Industrial Policy fell out of favor in the 1990s after the Washington Consensus, under which the International Monetary Fund (IMF) opposed tactics by states to control their own economic trajectory rather than letting market forces decide. Industrial Policy has seen a recent revival through policies like the US Inflation Reduction Act, the EU Chips Act, and Made in China Act 2025.

motions.motion: THBT China should use violent force to crackdown on organised crime in the Golden Triangle
motions.infoslide: The Golden Triangle is a hotbed of criminal activity at the border of Laos, China, Thailand, and Myanmar. It is one of the world's largest drug trafficking corridors both into and out of China, hosting scam centers, illegal gambling operations, and human trafficking dens. Most illicit activity is centered in a Special Economic Zone protected by Laos' government where large organised crime groups from Mainland China are allowed to operate casinos, some of which are rumored to have ties to high-ranking CCP officials. The Thai army polices the border both to curtail crime and to secure its territorial claim.

motions.motion: THW legalise child labour
This House prefers a world where Neros is widely available
motions.infoslide: Neros is a treatment which grants people the ability to choose when and to whom they experience attraction, as opposed to being attracted to people based on psychological factors outside of their control. Once a person undergoes Neros, they will always have to choose their attraction and cannot return to random, biological attraction.

motions.motion: This House prefers the production of modernised traditional fairytales to the production of new original stories for the same audience
motions.infoslide: For the purposes of this debate, modernised fairytales refer to traditionally popularised fairytale stories that have been altered in order to appeal to modern social norms, such as through changing the plot, characters or moral of the story.

motions.motion: This House believes that human rights organisations should focus on supporting the emigration of people who want to leave occupied territory (e.g. Palestine, Kashmir) instead of trying to end the occupation
motions.infoslide: Supporting the emigration of people who want to leave occupied territory would involve organising flights, money, and safety precautions for individuals who want to leave Palestine, for example.

motions.motion: This House believes that apoliticality is immoral
motions.infoslide: To be apolitical means to have no interest or involvement in political affairs.

motions.motion: This House believes that South Korea should re-open the Kaesong Industrial Complex
motions.infoslide: The Kaesong Industrial Complex provided South Korean companies with cheaper labor costs, while providing North Korea with critical hard currency.

motions.motion: This House believes that the queer community should focus on 'normalisation' instead of focusing on the uniqueness of the queer community
motions.infoslide: Normalisation involves promoting the narrative that queer people want to be 'normal' by getting married and having kids.

motions.motion: This House would allow corporations to conduct offensive cyber operations against criminal groups that target them using cyberwarfare (e.g. ransomware, hacks)
motions.infoslide: Offensive cyber includes techniques such as gaining access to target systems through hacking, exploiting vulnerabilities to disable digital or physical infrastructure, or deploying malicious software. This is not an exhaustive definition.

motions.motion: This House believes that Malaysia should prohibit vernacular schools
motions.infoslide: A vernacular school is one in which educational content is taught in a language other than the official one of the country.

motions.motion: TH opposes the narrative that true love never dies.
THW prohibit entertainment company from including terms and conditions restricting the personal lives of the employees within the working contracts (e.g. dating prohibition for celebrities, etc.).
TH supports youth in Northeast Asia embodying the 'Gen Z style' persona.
motions.infoslide: For the purpose of this debate, 'Gen Z style' refers to a disposition wherein an individual displays a set of characteristics that go against conventional social norms and communitarian values. Such style can include taking gap years during college instead of following a linear and uninterrupted education and career trajectory, refusing to submit to authorities (e.g. familial or workplace), and anti-establishment elements such as anti-consumerism.

motions.motion: TH prefers a world where presently autonomous North East Asian governments are replaced by one selected individual who is genetically modified to become a 'philosopher king' for the entire region.
motions.infoslide: In Plato's "The Republic," philosopher king refers to a hypothetical figure who melds profound philosophical knowledge and is intensively educated in disciplines like philosophy, ethics, and governance.

motions.motion: TH supports the use of 'short squeeze' tactics by retail investors in stock markets.
motions.infoslide: 'Short squeeze' occurs when a security has a significant amount of short sellers, meaning lots of investors are betting on its price falling. When investors see the price going up, they start to purchase back the stocks, which makes the price jump higher unexpectedly. An institutional investor trades large volumes of securities on behalf of an individual or shareholder. A retail investor is an individual who invests their own capital, typically at lower frequencies and volumes.

motions.motion: THBT the inception of the 'Mandatory Solidarity' mechanism is in the interest of the European Union.
motions.infoslide: The European Union (EU) adopted the 'Dublin Regulation' for handling irregular immigrants (e.g., immigrants entering a country through an informal process, including but not limited to asylum seekers). It stipulates that the countries where the immigrants arrive should handle the logistics, such as examining asylum claims and providing temporary shelters. This led to certain countries proximate to regions with a high population outflux bearing a disproportionately high burden, including Greece, Italy, and Malta (also known as 'front-line countries'). To remedy this, the EU is discussing the 'Mandatory Solidarity' mechanism, which relocates irregular immigrants from front-line countries to other EU countries and imposes financial penalties should countries reject the entry of immigrants. This measure is still being negotiated in the European Parliament and is yet to be ratified.

motions.motion: THBT hospitals should have the power to remove patients from the care of their legal guardians in suspected 'medical negligence' cases.
motions.infoslide: 'Medical negligence' refers to the failure of a carer to provide a standard level of care, resulting in harm or injury to a patient. This could include forgetting to administer medication, choosing a different treatment plan than what was prescribed, or breaching duty of care.

motions.motion: TH prefers 'Joint Legal Custody' as the primary norm for divorced families rather than 'Sole Legal Custody'.
motions.infoslide: In divorce law, there are two ways of settling a post-divorce parent-child relationship. In 'Sole Legal Custody', one parent has the exclusive authority to make significant decisions for the child, such as education, healthcare, and religion. The non-custodial parent typically has limited or no say in these decisions. In 'Joint Legal Custody', parents share the authority to make such decisions and financially contribute together, albeit one parent not being physically together with the child. Joint Legal Custody still set exceptions where a parent would not be granted custodial rights, such as previous history of physical/verbal abuse.

motions.motion: TH regrets the glamorisation of mental illness in the mainstream (i.e. media depiction of autistic geniuses).
TH opposes the trend of charitable organizations increasingly adopting 'resume padding' in their initiative and advocacy.
motions.infoslide: 'Resume padding' of charity refers to the strategic involvement of individuals, particularly students, in charitable activities with the primary goal of enhancing their resumes rather than a genuine desire to contribute to the cause. This practice has become increasingly common among students seeking to bolster their college applications, as admissions committees often view participation in charity work favourably.

motions.motion: THW significantly lessen the sentence for offenders who commit serious economic crimes if they can make up the loss to victims.
TH supports political parties in developing countries using post-colonial nationalism (e.g., BJP discussing changing the country's name to 'Bharat' in India).
TH supports the use of ‘Zombie Acting’.
motions.infoslide: 'Zombie acting' is the digital replication of a performer's voice or likeness to substantially manipulate a performance, create a new digital performance or train an artificial intelligence system designed to generate new visual, audio, or audiovisual content.

motions.motion: THBT China should aggressively shift the investment/industrial focus from coal industry to renewable energy industry.
THBT the LGBT movement in Taiwan should aggressively encourage closeted individuals to come out at 'Dà Zòng Jiǔ'.
motions.infoslide: 'Dà Zòng Jiǔ (大縱酒)' is a queer gathering held on a monthly basis in Taiwan. It has evolved into one of the most significant and biggest pride events with active participation from Twitter users within the LGBT community. Centered around extensive drinking, this event serves as a space where individuals from the LGBT community come together to enjoy alcohol and socialize with other people, alongside K-pop dancing, flirting/kissing people, and cosplaying.

motions.motion: THO the growing trend of psychedelic tourism.
motions.infoslide: Psychedelic retreats are wellness programs where participants use traditional psychoactive substances like psilocybin (magic mushrooms), ayahuasca, or peyote in guided, therapeutic settings to promote healing, self-discovery, and spiritual growth, typically led by shamans or trained facilitators. These retreats have historically attracted "patients" or "pilgrims" travelling to countries or communities where these traditional practices are legal and culturally accepted. Recently, such retreats have been promoted and popularised as tourist attractions.

motions.motion: In areas with high recidivism, THBT states should prioritise strengthening non-prison justice structures over directly reforming the prison system.
motions.infoslide: For the purposes of this debate, direct prison reforms include prison maintenance/construction, corrections staff training and wage policy, in- custody programmes, etc. In contrast, non-prison justice structures are criminal justice policies beyond prisons, including but are not limited to community justice, restoration and rehabilitation, sentencing and parole easing, etc.

motions.motion: TH prefers a world where Zou Hun is the dominant cultural norm.
motions.infoslide: "Zou Hun" (or Walking Marriage) is a traditional culture of the Mosuo people（摩梭人） that predominantly reside in the Yunnan and Sichuan province of China. It is a form of marriage in which the couple does not live together and only visits each other periodically, typically with the husband spending the night at the wife’s residence. Women are the heads of the household, and property is passed down from mother to daughter. The man and woman continue to live with their respective families and they do not share property or have joint financial responsibilities. Children within Zou Hun culture would be raised by the mother’s matrilineal family.

motions.motion: TH, as the Takaichi Administration, W recognise Palestine as a sovereign state.
motions.infoslide: Japan–Israel security and R&D cooperation has been developing for the past few decades and was strengthened by a 2015 visit from then‑PM Shinzo Abe, after which economic ties between the two countries grew substantially. Since October 7, the humanitarian crisis in Gaza has sparked significant public concern in Japan. According to 2025 survey data, Japan currently ranks among the countries with the highest levels of negative public sentiment toward Israel, with around three-quarters of adults holding unfavourable views of Israel and little or no confidence in Prime Minister Netanyahu.

motions.motion: THW force all insular religious communities to have their members embark on a "Rumspringa" upon reaching legal adulthood.
motions.infoslide: Rumspringa is a tradition in some Amish communities, typically beginning around age 16. Amish youth are given the chance to engage with non-Amish society (e.g. adopt modern clothing and technology, explore life beyond their community’s strict norms, etc.) for a period of time, before deciding whether to be baptised into the Amish church.

motions.motion: TH, as Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, W actively seek to resume nuclear talks with the U.S.
motions.infoslide: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is the supreme leader of Iran.

motions.motion: Assuming global feasibility, THW restrict the conduct of all Dual Use Research of Concern to occur only within the public sector.
motions.infoslide: Dual Use Research of Concern refers to research whose outcomes are perceived to be either immensely beneficial for human civilization or devastating for populations when misapplied. Current examples include, but are not limited to: gain-of-function research in which pathogens are modified to be more infective, genetic drives where engineered organisms are released into the wild, etc.

motions.motion: TH welcomes the rise of "red-chip" art.
motions.infoslide: Red-chip art refers to art from emerging or mid-career artists whose work gains rapid popularity and high market value, often through social media, pop culture relevance, and online demand. This is in contrast to blue-chip art, denoting works by established artists with a sustained record of visibility and recognition, typically supported by traditional institutions such as galleries, dealers, and major exhibitions.
motions.round: English as a Foreign Language Finals

motions.motion: THS queer opacity in aesthetic expression.
motions.infoslide: Queer opacity is a concept that values the unknowability or indeterminacy of queer identities, resisting the demand to be fully understood or made legible within dominant social norms.

motions.motion: THR the rise of optimisation culture.
motions.infoslide: The "optimisation culture" refers to a mindset focused on maximising output for minimum input, which has become a defining feature of modern society. This is reflected in the global popularity of terms that evaluate cost-performance, such as "性价比(Xìngjiàbǐ)" in China, "가성비(Gaseongbi)" in Korea, and "コスパ (Kosupa)" in Japan. This culture of "optimisation" is applied to a wide range of situations, from mundane purchases when shopping to major life choices like careers, education, hobbies, and even relationships, framing all aspects of life as a problem of efficiency to be solved.

motions.motion: TH, as the feminist movement in the developing world, supports the glorification of hustle culture perperated by women.

In art and popular culture, THBT creating new lead characters for minorities (eg Hancock, Black Panther) is better than recasting them in existing roles already played by other actors/communities (eg Ghostbusters, The Little Mermaid).
THBT that BookTube and BookTok has done more harm to reading culture than good
THO the rise of disassociative feminism.
motions.infoslide: A disassociative feminist conveys feminine struggles more passively (internalizing pain, joking about their struggles, taking a sarcastic tone, etc) rather than actively complaining about them. This category of feminism is ‘detached’ and ‘disconnected’ from more active forms of feminist resistance, such as the girl boss phenomenon or street protests. Disassociative feminism recognizes the emotional and physical toll that comes with active resistance and efforts that take to break the glass ceilings. A dissociative feminist would also embrace life choices that aren’t typically feminist in nature. Example: Fleabag from Fleabag, Rue from Euphoria, etc

motions.motion: TH, as a feminist living in KSA, supports the purple washing of Vision 2030.
motions.infoslide: According to the government of Saudi Arabia, Vision 2030 “is a unique transformative economic and social reform blueprint that is opening Saudi Arabia up to the world.” As part of the project, the government has started several projects such as building smart cities, sports complexes, renewable energy projects, tourism programs, the entertainment industry, and so on. In an effort to diversify its economy and attract foreign investments, the government is also focusing on gender equality. Recently, the government also lifted restrictions on women’s right to drive, gave access to sports events, etc. Purple washing is defined as appealing to women rights and gender equality for political or economic objectives.

motions.motion: In its messaging during conservation efforts, TH believes that the environmental movement should prioritize the economic worth of the environment over its cultural worth to humans
THBT Just Stop Oil protests have done more harm than good.
motions.infoslide: Just Stop Oil is a UK-based environmental activist group that aims at halting oil production and licensing by the British government. The group is known for its civil resistance approach to protesting, which includes but is not limited to throwing soup on famous valuable paintings, gluing themselves to artwork in museums, tying themselves to goalposts during football matches, etc.

motions.motion: In the discourse of climate action, THS the narrative that we are all going to die.
THBT that writing POC protagonists (e.g. Devi in Never Have I Ever) with white love interests does more harm than good
As a young person in their 20s, THP situationships over romantic relationships.
TH, as a debater, would make an active decision to not date a fellow debater.
As opposed to military neutrality, THS Sweden's and Finland's application to join NATO.
In hindsight, THR the 2019 coup against Evo Morales of Bolivia.
motions.infoslide: Evo Morales was the democratically elected President of Bolivia from 2006 to 2019. As the first indigenous Prime Minister of a country that holds one of the largest indigenous populations in the world, Evo Morales was responsible for important reforms such as elevating extreme poverty by 60 percent. However, during the election for Evo’s fourth term, he was accused of rigging the voter process to find an early win in elections by the army. In the light of violent anti-Morales protests that followed, Evo resigned in the hopes of avoiding complete civil unrest. Other important claims were about the long-termed tenure of Evo Morales and his policy of Movement Towards Socialism, which was also described as socialist autocracy by his opponents. After the ousting of Evo Morales’ democratic government, the demand for more democratic and liberal structures increased in the country. The citizens became more critical of the army as well as the Organization of American State, a US-backed policy intervention entity that maintains the democratic pursuits of the west. At the same time, the citizens of Bolivia, especially the indigenous populations, became more supportive of the Pink Tide i.e. a left-wing political wave in Latin America.

THBT academics should not include the scholastically-important works of individuals with immoral values in their curriculum (eg. anti-semite Wagner in a music course, enthusiastic slaveholder Jonathan Edwards in a philosophy course, Nazi collaborator and African filmmaker / photojournalist Riefenstahl in an anthropology course, etc.)
THBT educational institutions should heavily de-emphasize the value of academic excellence (eg: Using Pass/fail marking, abolishing distinctions or letter grading)

motions.infoslide: Mark is a Sophomore at a well-reputed college. He has a product idea that is unique, useful, and caters to a wide market gap. He believes his revolutionary product has the potential to change the landscape of its industry.

In the systems of criminal justice by a jury, THW adopt the veil of ignorance.
motions.infoslide: For the purpose of this debate, the veil of ignorance is a component of the social contract that is supposed to ensure the impartiality of judgment. If adopted, the veil will make everyone in the criminal justice system blind to each other’s personal, historical, and social characteristics. This includes lacking clues to their class, their privileges, their disadvantages, or even their personality.

motions.motion: THR the narrative that doctors are heroes.
TH as person X would pull the lever
motions.infoslide: A Zookeeper forgot to close the gates to Osaka Zoo, and 40-50 animals have escaped to the nearby train station. Mary, searches for them on track A while the animals are on track B. All of a sudden, a highspeed Shinkansen (train) heading to track A emerges at 320 km/h. Person X, spots a nearby lever and knows for certain pulling the lever would result in the Shinkansen going through track B instead of track A. The Shinkansen passing through either track would mean that no one or no animal would be able to escape due to the high velocity of the train, and there are no alternative methods to divert or stop the train (including alerting the zookeeper or the animals)

motions.motion: THW heist the zoo

Both leaders have significant foreign support in terms of money and arms. Countries like Egypt, Eritrea, Ukraine and Iran support the SAF while the RSF has ties with UAE, Russia, etc.

Pro-democracy see them as war criminals for their role in counter-insurgency in Darfur in 2003 as well as recent escalations in Khartoum. The RSF is attempting to secure control over oil refineries to limit the government's access to the oil industry and its profits. Sudan's biggest source of income is its oil, with countries like China, India, and Japan being its biggest buyers. ﻿﻿

motions.motion: THR the categorization of personality traits (green flags, red flags, the icks, etc) in modern-day dating.
TH, as X & Y, would choose to debate with each other.
motions.infoslide: Context: 1) X & Y are budding debaters from the Karachi circuit who are in a happy relationship. They share common aspirations of doing well at major tournaments (WUDC, UADC etc.) and have briefly debated as a team previously. However, this took a significant toll on their relationship since both of them are highly-competitive teammates prone to bouts of stress and anger. Based on this experience, they have teamed up extensively with a different set of teammates which has been successful. For the purposes of this debate, this is likely to be the last year majors are going to be online, post which it is uncertain whether they will be able to access these tournaments. X & Y have spent time preparing for majors with their current teammates. However, in the run up to majors, they realize their current teammates won't be able to make it to the competition. This leaves them with the choice of either debating with each other or settling for an untested partner. Both X & Y are still quite skeptical of the potential fallout teaming up may have on their relationship.

motions.motion: This house believes that government should predominantly base its policies on the country’s Gross National Happiness
motions.infoslide: Gross National Happiness (GNH) is a philosophy that guides the government of Bhutan. It includes an index which is used to measure the collective happiness and well-being of a population. The GNH Index includes nine domains: 1) psychological well being 2) health 3) education 4) time use 5) cultural diversity and resilience 6) good governance 7) community vitality 8) ecological diversity and resilience 9) living standards.

motions.motion: THP 'apocalyptic speculative fiction' over 'optimistic speculative fiction.'
motions.infoslide: For the purpose of this debate, speculative fiction refers to any genre of art that depicts the future of science, technology, religion, culture, etc. In other words, such pieces of art speculate on something supposedly 'nonexistent' within our reality. Within this genre, there is 'apocalyptic speculative fiction', which shows our eventual doom, such as Dune or The Handmaid's Tale. And there is 'optimistic speculative fiction' which shows an 'optimistic' side of our future: we will all be alright. Example: 'The Long Way to a Small, Angry Planet.'

motions.motion: This house would prefer to experience art knowing nothing of the artist.
This House, as an author from a minority background writing science fiction and fantasy media, would choose to write using the re-interpretive approach rather than the transformative approach
motions.infoslide: For the purposes of this debate, there are two approaches to writing fantasy and science fiction media. The "re-interpretive approach" directly reinterprets or draws strong parallels to real historical events (e.g. Outlander, The Poppy War, Belisarius). Conversely, the "transformative approach" takes inspiration from real world cultures but builds an otherwise unique world (e.g. Avatar: The Last Airbender, Dune, The Wheel of Time)

motions.motion: TH, as a feminist, would look for a man in finance, trust fund, 6'5, blue eyes.
motions.infoslide: For the purpose of this debate, 'looking for a man in finance' trend refers to womxn actively seeking to date rich men who work in lucrative jobs or have generational wealth. That's largely because to have a fun dating life, for example, flying out to Paris for a date. Such womxn prioritize wealth and looks in their partners, ignoring their annoying traits like mansplaining, very capitalist views, or just love for finance.

motions.motion: This house believes that true womxn's liberation is incompatible with the existence of organized religion
This house, as the progressive glacier movement, would campaign for glaciers to acquiesce to global warming voluntarily.
motions.infoslide: "For the purposes of this debate, glaciers (e.g. ice sheets, icebergs, ice shelves) have suddenly developed sentience, communication and the capacity to consciously control the pace of their melting or stop it entirely. Glaciers cannot stop the process of global warming or its other adverse consequences (e.g. sea levels may rise at a different pace, but this does not affect carbon emissions or the prevalence of bushfires). Nobody else apart from the glacier species is aware that glaciers have developed intelligent thought.”

motions.motion: THR the trend of building climate justice movements around one popular figure (Greta Thunberg or Roger Hallam, the founder of Just Stop Oil)
THP shareholder activism to divestment campaigns in the pursuit of environmental activism
motions.infoslide: Shareholder activism involves the owners of shares (whether individual or institutional investors) lobbying companies in which they have shares to improve their corporate behaviour. Divestment campaigns involve demanding investors to sell their shares in morally problematic industries.

motions.motion: This house prefers US federal reserve-driven bail-outs as opposed to legislative-driven bail-outs.
motions.infoslide: a) In the U.S., Federal Reserve-driven bailouts involve the Fed (Federal reserve) providing emergency loans, credit facilities, or monetary support to stabilize financial institutions and markets during crises. These actions aim to maintain liquidity and prevent systemic collapse. b) Legislative-driven bailouts occur when Congress passes laws to allocate taxpayer funds for rescuing struggling industries or companies. These bailouts focus on broader economic recovery and often include conditions like repayment or operational reforms.

motions.motion: THR the creation of the Social Security Trust fund
motions.infoslide: Rather than the government directly funding social security benefits, the US government established the ‘Social Security Trust Fund’ which is responsible for paying out social security benefits. By law, the trust fund is funded by a dedicated payroll tax of 12.4%. The trust fund is required to invest surpluses into US Treasury Bonds for the payment of future Social Security benefits. Payroll taxes currently do ﻿not cover the full cost of social security, and the trust funds are burning through their cash reserves. The trust funds are expected to be depleted by 2034, at which point payroll taxes will only cover 77% of the cost of social security.

motions.motion: THBT it is in the interest of queer folks to commercialize queer-dominant spaces (drag shows, pride marches, gay bars, trans nightclubs)

motions.infoslide: You're Achilles, one of the greatest warriors that Greece has ever seen. Your destiny is written. Your role in winning the Trojan War for your home, Prithia, will be monumental. However, it is certain that you will die a physical death that will render you as a great Greek Hero. But you also have a choice to stay home, and build your life with Patroclus, your male lover. If you choose to stay in Prithia and not fight the war, you will become the first Greek warrior to forge the greatest same-sex love story in Greece.

The effects are temporary and will last at least a year, though it's unclear at what point after that they will fade away. Animals used in the testing process did not experience negative side effects. Algernon is a woman with a well below- average IQ whose daily material needs are met. She is given the chance to be the first human to try the drug.

motions.motion: This house hopes for the message to confirm the existence of a deterministic universe
motions.infoslide: For the purposes of this debate, humanity has achieved communication with a metaphysical being possessing knowledge about the universe's nature. The being sends a message enclosed in an envelope, offering insight into whether historical events and human existence are predetermined or shaped in real-time by individuals. The being is deemed trustworthy, and the information in the message is considered accurate and reliable.

In this system, the city is in charge of providing basic services like education and healthcare. No biological child-parent records are maintained although they are allowed to interact with the outside world.

motions.motion: This House would require major tech companies working on generative AI to go open source.
motions.infoslide: Generative AI refers to artificial intelligence capable of generating text, images, or other media, using generative models. Generative AI models learn the patterns and structure of their input training data and then generate new data that has similar characteristics. Examples of generative AI include large language model chatbots such as ChatGPT and Bard, text-to-image artificial intelligence art systems such as Midjourney, and DALL-E, and text-to-video artificial intelligence systems like Sora.

motions.infoslide: For this motion: There is no way to change the date of your death even after learning this motion.

motions.motion: This House would create UN protectorates in falling states.
motions.infoslide: A UN protectorate is a state who has ceded autonomy over defense and other law violations to the United Nations. Previous examples include Kosovo and East Timor.

motions.motion: This House, as the government of India, should withdraw from the WTO.
motions.infoslide: In light of the three farm acts passed by the Government of India in September 2020, farmers across the country went on a year-long protest which ended in December 2021. In February 2024, more than 30 farmer unions launched the 'Dilli Chalo' protest demanding a minimum support price for farmers. The World Trade Organization is an international body which facilitates the trade between member countries through the reduction of tariffs and other protectionist regulations.

motions.motion: This House prefers a world with this procedure.
motions.infoslide: For the purposes of this debate, a procedure has been invented which allows individuals to erase specific memories from their mind. It has no side effects or long term consequences besides the erasure of the specific memory. It works with 100% accuracy but is completely irreversible.

motions.motion: This house supports the establishment of a central authority within social movements.
This house regrets the prominence of left/liberal views at universities.
This house believes that microcredit does more harm than good.
motions.infoslide: Microcredit involves providing small loans to individuals, typically in developing countries, who lack access to traditional banking services by lowering eligibility requirements, no collateral, and smaller loaning amounts. Microcredit programs often target women and marginalized communities.

motions.motion: This house prefers a world where all sports are played in the GAA mode.
motions.infoslide: Hurling is a Gaelic sport native to Ireland, governed by the Gaelic Athletic Association. The structure of the GAA is a voluntary, democratic association, with a base of workers being volunteers with voting rights. The individual club is the basic unit of the association, with fans and volunteers owning the vast majority stake and no private ownership or buyouts of clubs. The viewership rights of games are held by the GAA. Profit made from tournaments goes to grassroots organisations, clubs, and staff in order to train young players for the sport. Sponsors for Hurling include Ford, Allianz etc, and the most recent All Ireland Hurling Championship final had a viewing of more than half a million.

motions.motion: This house would put a cap on financial and energy capital expenditure each company can allocate to the development of AI.
This house predicts that China will have the largest economy by GDP in 50 years time.

motions.infoslide: You are a religious practicing Muslim person in India and are set to marry a religious practicing Hindu partner. You have decided to have and raise children in India after marriage.

motions.motion: This house prefers a world where familial care labour is seen as a distinct service job rather than a personal/private responsibility.
motions.infoslide: Familial care labour describes work like household chores, household planning and coordination.

motions.motion: This house, as the World Bank, would aggressively fund models of conservation economics.
motions.infoslide: Conservation economics values ecosystem services in economic terms attaching monetary value to nature, It uses cost-benefit analysis and market-based tools like carbon pricing to incentivize environmental protection. The Yasuni ITT initiative is a key example, where Ecuador sought international funding to leave oil reserves in a bio reserve untapped at the cost of economic opportunities in exchange for conservation.

motions.motion: THBT treating sobriety as a moral virtue is unjustified.

motions.infoslide: Latin American countries have experienced a decline in traditional Catholicism, indicated by the growth of Protestant and Pentecostal communities. Protestant identifiers increased from 4% in 1970 to nearly 20% by 2014. This has also overlapped with a surge in the number of individuals rejecting institutional religion altogether.

motions.motion: THBT humanity should accept the deal.
motions.infoslide: A sentient alien species offers humanity unlimited knowledge in exchange for complete planetary control over Earth.

motions.infoslide: Multi-club ownership (MCO) in football is a rapidly growing model where a single entity significantly controls or influences multiple clubs.

motions.motion: THO the Reserve Bank of India's move to legalise leveraged buyouts.
motions.infoslide: A leveraged buyout (LBO) is a form of acquisition in which a company is purchased primarily using borrowed funds, with the debt typically repaid from the future cash flows or assets of the acquired firm. In 2026, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) changed its rules to allow Indian banks to finance company takeovers, including deals that rely heavily on leveraged buyouts. Formerly, banks were restricted from directly funding such acquisitions.

motions.motion: In states with high levels of incarceration, THW make federal funding for state prisons conditional upon achieving decarceration targets
motions.infoslide: For the purposes of this debate, decarceration targets refer to measurable goals that individual states set for themselves to reduce their incarcerated populations within a certain timeframe.

motions.motion: THBT the human race is entitled to anything they discover or invent.
THS the narrative that citizens of former colonial powers bear a moral responsibility for their colonial past regardless of generation.

THW issue a Parent License
motions.infoslide: There is an ongoing discussion on whether the State should regulate who gets to have children and start a family. Famous propositions that push this are the issuance of a Parent License to deserving couples based on a state- mandated set of standards.

motions.motion: THO Antinatalism
motions.infoslide: Antinatalism is the belief that procreation is morally wrong and that people should be encouraged not to have children. It stems mainly from the discussion on how bringing a child into this world means that they will inevitably experience pain and suffering from different aspects of life (poverty, political tensions, environmental degradation, emotional and mental burdens, etc.).

motions.motion: THW only allow surrogacy in cases of medical necessity
THS the emphasis on Realism as a guiding theory for policymaking in post-colonial states
motions.infoslide: Realism as a theory in International Relations emphasizes 2 things: (1) states are inherently self-interested and will prioritize their own domestic security, and (2) there is no global governing institution that can restrain or keep states in check.

motions.motion: THBT ASEAN should abandon the “ASEAN way” in dealing with conflicts between its member-states.
motions.infoslide: The ASEAN believes in non-interference and quiet diplomacy that’s why all decisions made by ASEAN are consensus-based. This means that all member-states need to come to an agreement before a decision is ratified. This framework is called the “ASEAN way”. There have been multiple instances of ASEAN member states being in conflict with each other (i.e Thailand-Cambodia border disputes, PH-Vietnam-Malaysia-Brunei overlapping claims over the South China Sea, etc). In these instances, ASEAN takes a neutral observer position in respect to the principle of non-interference and the ASEAN way.

motions.motion: THBT non-state actors (ie movements, Indigenous groups, religious groups, etc) should be given seats at the United Nations General Assembly
THBT the ornamental fish industry has done more harm than good in developing countries.
motions.infoslide: The ornamental fish industry is the global trade of fish and other aquatic animals kept for decorative aquariums, sourced through both captive breeding and wild capture. Most freshwater ornamental fish are bred in captivity, while many marine species are wild-caught.

motions.motion: This House supports regulated trophy hunting as a conservation tool.
motions.infoslide: Regulated trophy hunting is the controlled hunting of certain animal species under government or wildlife authority rules. Hunters typically pay fees or obtain licenses that limit the number and type of animals taken. This practice is often implemented in regions with populations of large or iconic wildlife, such as Africa and North America.

motions.motion: This House would restrict livestock production in regions facing severe environmental degradation.
THBT all elementary teachers should be required to take psychological assessments.
motions.infoslide: Psychological assessments are standardized tests used to evaluate a person’s cognitive abilities, personality traits, and emotional functioning. They are commonly used in education, employment, and clinical settings to understand strengths, and weaknesses.

motions.motion: THR the heavy emphasis of "resilience" in basic education teaching.
THBT Filipino Pride has done more harm than good.
motions.infoslide: Filipino pride refers to a sense of national identity and pride in Filipino culture, achievements, and heritage. It celebrates the country’s history, values, arts, and resilience, fostering unity and recognition of the Philippines on both local and global stages.

motions.motion: THR the normalization of "utang na loob".
motions.infoslide: Utang na loob is a Filipino social value centered on gratitude and reciprocity, where individuals feel a moral obligation to return favors or kindness received. It promotes social cohesion and mutual support, but also raises broader questions about pressure, obligation, and fairness in personal, political, and institutional relationships.

motions.motion: THO the over-celebration of Filipino resilience.
THBT Microfinance Institutions have failed in the Philippines
motions.infoslide: Microfinance Institutions (MFIs) are financial organizations that provide banking services to low-income individuals or groups who are otherwise excluded from traditional banking systems due to poverty, lack of collateral, or unverifiable credit history. Notable examples of MFIs include Ramon Aboitiz Foundation Inc. Microfinance (RAFI MFI), Center for Agriculture and Rural Development (CARD), Negros Women for Tomorrow foundation (NWTF), and others.

motions.motion: THBT developing states should abolish taxes on green technologies (i.e., E-Vehicles, Solar Energy Panels, etc.)
TH, as a developing economy, W actively prioritize the establishment of cooperatives over corporations.
motions.infoslide: A Cooperative is a business structure owned and democratically controlled by its members who use or work for the organization. Unlike corporations, voting power is equal (""one member, one vote"") regardless of investment size, and profits are returned to members based on usage rather than share ownership. This model prioritizes member welfare and local community reinvestment. A Corporation is a business structure owned and financially controlled by shareholders who invest capital in the organization. Unlike cooperatives, voting power is proportional to wealth ('one share, one vote') regardless of active participation, and profits are returned to shareholders based on ownership stake rather than usage. This model prioritizes maximizing shareholder value and return on investment.

motions.motion: TH, as the developing state, would put premium on the adoption of 15-minute city model for all future urban development.
motions.infoslide: The 15-minute city is an urban planning model where residents can access essential daily needs—work, housing, food, health, education, and culture—within a 15-minute walk or bike ride from their home.

motions.motion: THBT environmental protection should not be sacrificed for development.
THS the privatization of third spaces in developing states.
motions.infoslide: Third spaces are social environments outside of home and the workplace where people gather, connect, and build community. This looks like cafés, community centers, parks, libraries, and coworking spaces.

motions.motion: THW publicize this discovery.
motions.infoslide: You discover a new realm that is completely untainted with any human activity and contains a wide variety of unknown organisms. Its inhabitants are non- human and have a perfect symbiotic relationship with their environment where their existence does not harm their realm at all.

motions.motion: THR the rise of the “Deserve Ko' To” mentality among Filipino consumers.
motions.infoslide: The "Deserve Ko 'To" mentality is a cultural phenomenon where Filipino consumers justify spending on non-essentials (premium coffee, gadgets, or travel) as a necessary emotional reward for their hard work and daily struggles.

motions.motion: Assuming feasibility, THW penalize individuals who fail to consistently track their personal finances (i.e., listing down their expenses for the day).
TH, as a middle-class individual, W prioritize investing in stocks over bonds.
motions.infoslide: Stocks represent a fractional ownership interest in a corporation. When you purchase a share, you become a partial owner (shareholder) with a claim on the company's assets and potential earnings. Bonds function as a loan from an investor to a borrower, such as a corporation or government. When you buy a bond, you become a creditor, and the bond certificate. The issuer is legally obligated to pay you a fixed rate of interest (coupon) at regular intervals and return the principal amount (face value) on a specific maturity date.

motions.motion: THO the rise of schools actively celebrating pride month
motions.infoslide: Pride Month, observed every June, recognizes the history, identities, and ongoing struggles of LGBTQ+ communities, promoting values of equality, dignity, and inclusion. It serves both as a celebration of diversity and a reminder of the continuing need to address discrimination and unequal treatment in society.

motions.motion: THS the creation of women spaces only in universities
motions.infoslide: Women-only spaces refer to environments created to support women’s safety, dignity, and participation by addressing historical and structural inequalities. They are often justified as a means of protection, empowerment, and inclusion, while also raising broader debates about equality, access, and how societies balance competing rights.

motions.motion: THBT child beauty pageants has done more harm than good (e.g. Baby Miss, Little Miss, Petite Miss)
THR the romanticization of “organic encounter”
motions.infoslide: An organic encounter in dating is the act of meeting a potential partner naturally in the real world, without the assistance of apps, websites, or algorithms. These meetings typically occur spontaneously during everyday life, such as being introduced by a mutual friend, striking up a conversation at a coffee shop, or connecting through a shared hobby.

motions.infoslide: Malaysia was formed on 16 September 1963 by the merger of the Federation of Malaya with the former British colonies of North Borneo, Sarawak, and Singapore. Disagreements on revenue sharing by Singapore and the failure to set up the common market, and the hostile political relations between the People's Action Party (PAP) and United Malays National Organisation (UMNO) on the topic of Article 153 and Malaysian Malaysia increased racial tensions and culminated to the 1964 Race Riots in Singapore. On 7 August 1965, Prime Minister Tunku Abdul Rahman lead a Parliament vote to expel Singapore from Malaysia, citing the incompatibility and to reduce further racial tension and bloodshed.

Abiy Ahmed, an Oromo (The Majority ethnicity) Muslim rose as the Prime Minister of Ethiopia campaigning for an end to ethno-federalism and a centralised Ethiopian government instead. Ethiopia has been involved in an ongoing conflict with the Tigray People's Liberation Front, representing the Tigray area and ethnicity regarding the freedom and potential independence of the Tigray people.

motions.motion: THBT the UN should intervene in Sudan on behalf of the civilian government
motions.infoslide: The conflict in Sudan is one between the Sudanese army, led by Abdel Fattah and the para militia group Rapid Support Forces (RSF), led by Hemedti. Together, they overthrew Omar al-Bashir, Sudan’s dictator of 30 years and then organised another military coup against the post-Bashir civillian government they installed. They currently have a power-sharing agreement but they are disputing about the integration of RSF into the military, and how succession and rankings would work in the new system. This has led to growing tensions, militarization and thus conflict between both parties in cities like Khartoum and Darfour.

motions.motion: THS the Creation of the East African Federation
motions.infoslide: The East African Federation (EFA) is a proposed political union of the seven sovereign states of the East African Community in the African Great Lakes region – Burundi, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Kenya, Rwanda, South Sudan, Tanzania and Uganda – as a single federated sovereign state. The EFA would be the biggest and most populous as well as the 4th largest GDP in Africa, and all of them have adopted Swahili as a national language, though different languages, religions, and ethnicities exists in the EFA.

motions.motion: THBT the narrative of needing a romantic relationship for the pursuit of happiness has done more harm than good
THR the emphasis on being independent
motions.infoslide: For the purposes of this debate, "independent" refers to self-reliance in all aspects of life, and is not only limited to relationships.

motions.motion: THS the rise of polygamy in modern relationships
TH as a Company, would embrace the Friedman Doctrine
motions.infoslide: The Friedman Doctrine holds that decisions concerning social responsibility does not lie on the executives of a company. It believes that companies are not obligated to any social responsibilities unless the shareholders decide so, and is only obligated in pursuing profits for the company.

motions.motion: THW abolish the US debt ceiling
motions.infoslide: The US debt ceiling is a limit that Congress places on the presidency as to how much it is allowed to borrow in order to finance the annual budget.

motions.motion: THP the bailout of failing banks rather than the sale of them to bigger banks
This house believes that Malaysia should adopt Singapore's Certificate of Entitlement (COE) system to address traffic congestion effectively
motions.infoslide: The Certificate of Entitlement (COE) system is a system that controls the growth of the vehicle population in Singapore. It requires aspiring vehicle owners to bid for a COE through an online open auction. The COE price therefore reflects the amount that buyers are willing to pay to own a new car, private bus, goods vehicle or motorcycle. The COE gives private car owner the right to own and use the vehicle for 10 years, after which the owner can choose to de-register the vehicle or renew the COE.

motions.motion: This house believes that individuals should be financially incentivized for adopting sustainable lifestyles and making environmentally-friendly choices
This house supports the legalization of geoengineering as a means to mitigate the effects of climate change
motions.infoslide: Geoengineering refers to deliberate large-scale interventions in the Earth's climate system to counteract or mitigate the effects of climate change. It involves the deliberate manipulation of the Earth's atmosphere, oceans, or land surface to reduce the impacts of global warming. Geoengineering techniques can be broadly categorized into two main approaches which are Solar Radiation Management (SRM) and Carbon Dioxide Removal (CDR). SRM is techniques aim to reduce the amount of sunlight reaching the Earth's surface, thereby cooling the planet. Whereas, CDR is a techniques aim to remove carbon dioxide (CO2) from the atmosphere to reduce greenhouse gas concentrations.

motions.motion: THR the glorification of debate in politics
THR the over-emphasis of analytical rigor in competitive debates
motions.infoslide: For the purpose of this debate, analytical rigor means a critical and logical focus on argumentation and analysis

motions.motion: THBT AP is a better format than BP
THW bring back the jury system in Malaysian Courts
THW Determine a Person's Ethical or Legal Understanding as an Aggravating Factor in the Criminal Justice System
THW remove victim impact statements from the criminal justice system
motions.infoslide: A Victim Impact Statement is a written or oral statement presented to the court at the sentencing of the defendant. Many times victims, their family members, and friends of the victim participate in both written and verbal statements. Victim Impact Statements were created as an opportunity for the judge to hear how a criminal action has affected the victim's loved ones.

motions.motion: THW Ban Nationally Owned or Nationally Linked Organisations from Owning and Sponsoring Sports Clubs
THW Ban Nations Which Refuse to Compete Against Other Specific Nations (e.g Malaysia & Israel, Ukraine & Russia) from competing in International Sports
THBT TMJ's Purchase and Development of JDT Has Caused More Harm Than Good
motions.infoslide: The Tunku Mahkota Johor (TMJ), Crown Prince of Johor bought the Johor Football team in 2012. Since then, The rebranded Johor Darul Ta'zim (JDT) has achieved success in Malaysia and Asia, becoming the first Southeast Asian team to win a continental title and reaching the Round of 16 of the AFC Champions League. The team has done so by developing and changing local football while signing the best local and international players from other Malaysian teams.

motions.motion: This House Regrets the Blurred Lines ruling
motions.infoslide: In 2015, a court ruled that Robin Thicke's song, Blurred Lines was found to be plagiarising Marvin Gaye's Got To Give It Up. It set a landmark ruling as it plagiarised Got To Give It Up's "feel and groove" even though it was in a different key and had a different chord progression. Before the ruling, sampling without consent is already considered plagiarism. Since then, many other artists such as Dua Lipa, Olivia Rodrigo, and Ed Sheeran have been sued over plagiarism on similar lines.

This house would grant writers ownership rights over their original characters and storylines, even when working under the employment of studios or streaming platforms
THBT EMILY's List should also fund pro-choice male candidates
motions.infoslide: EMILY's List is an American political action committee (PAC) that aims to help elect Democratic female candidates in favor of abortion rights to office.

motions.motion: THBT Linda Yaccarino should not have accepted the CEO role at Twitter
motions.infoslide: Linda Yaccarino is the new CEO of Twitter and X Corp. She previously worked in the Donald Trump and Joe Biden administration and chaired the future of work task force in the World Economic Forum, an organisation which Elon Musk has openly criticised. She has resigned from her position as head of NBCUniversal's Head of Advertising Sales for her current position.

motions.motion: This house believes that quotas should be implemented to ensure gender parity in corporate boardrooms.

Assuming that the Earth encountered extraterrestrial life, TH as the human race, would avoid all contact with them and keep our existence a secret insofar as they possess or have discovered space travel for themselves.

THR the overuse of zero to hero trope in medias
motions.infoslide: The zero to hero trope is a theme where the main character (MC) obtains certain capacity (superpower, items, skills, etc.) that completely changes the course of their life. This newfound capacity then becomes narrative device that propels the plot of the story, and any character development subsequently revolves around it

motions.motion: TH, as China, would actively support the resistance movement in Myanmar
motions.infoslide: The Three Brotherhood Alliance in Myanmar is an alliance of ethnic minority insurgent groups. Alongside pro-democracy fighters and the Leople's Defence Forces (backed by the National Unity Government), they have reportedly seized over 300 bases and several towns, and have been described as the biggest battlefield challenge to the junta's rule since the coup. China has not officially supported either side of the conflict, opting instead to broker peace talks between the rebels and the junta.

motions.motion: TH celebrates the fall of X (formerly known as Twitter)​
THW MANDATE PROVEN PROFITABILITY AS A PREREQUISITE FOR COMPANIES TO ISSUE AN IPO​
motions.infoslide: An IPO is an initial public offering. In an IPO, a privately owned company lists its shares on a stock exchange, making them available for purchase by the general public as a means of fundraising; colloquially this is known as 'going public'​

motions.infoslide: The "Reductio Emotionum" is a device that is capable of limiting the intensity of emotions an individual may feel (this includes both pleasure and pain).

THBT states in ongoing civil conflicts should offer amnesty to defectors of insurgent groups in exchange for them agreeing to serve in the military

This house believes that feminists in Muslim-majority countries should advocate for the abolishment of Sharia Courts rather than working to reform it (e.g. advocating for more female judges, pushing for feminist interpretations of the Quran)
motions.infoslide: Sharia refers to Islamic religious law. Many countries have dual-legal systems, with Sharia courts existing alongside secular courts.

motions.motion: THBT local city governments in major metropolitan cities should prioritise infilling existing neighbourhoods as opposed to creating new neighbourhoods
motions.infoslide: Infilling refers to the process of building new housing in an already established neighbourhood, such as: -putting up a row of town houses where one or more single -family homes stood previously-constructing a new building on a vacant lot Note: Metropolitan cities are cities with a population of more than 4 million, e.g. Delhi, Shanghai.

motions.motion: THR the glorification of the "starving artist"
motions.infoslide: A starving artist is an artist who sacrifices material well-being in order to focus on their artwork. Some starving artists desire mainstream success but have difficulty doing so. These artists frequently take temporary positions such as waitering or other service industry jobs while they focus their attention on "breaking through" in their preferred field.

motions.motion: THBT that language education in schools should be descriptive rather than prescriptive.
motions.infoslide: Prescriptive approaches to language typically impose what is the "correct" way to use language, including but not limited to, grammar, syntax etc. Descriptive approaches to language aims to teach language through observing and describing how language can be used differently, across speakers and contexts and are more accepting of varieties eg. African American Vernacular English, Singlish etc.

motions.motion: THW significantly relax graduation requirements for schools in underprivileged areas (e.g. separate standardised testing, relaxed attendance requirements, flexible submission deadlines, reduced number of subjects, etc.)

THBT the EU should cut funding* to EU member states that violate Article 2 of the EU Treaty *eg: Agriculture subsidies
motions.infoslide: Article 2 of the Treaty on European Union sets out the conditions that member states must uphold in order to members of the EU - namely “the values of respect for human dignity, freedom, democracy, equality, the rule of law, and respect for human rights” etc. However, over the past decade anti-democratic tendencies have been identified in the young Central and Eastern European member states. Erosions of democracy have progressed furthest in Hungary and Poland: In Hungary, the concentration of power under Prime Minister Victor Orbán has steadily increased since 2010, and in 2020, in response to the coronavirus pandemic, the Hungarian Parliament approved a bill to make the state of emergency indefinite and grant the ability for Prime Minister Orbán to rule by decree. Poland has also seen continuous erosions such as measures that undermine the independence of the judiciary, allow the ruling party to dominate the media, and silence criticism from civil society. Threats to media freedom were seen in Czech Republic and Slovakia in 2018 and Croatia, Lithuania, Bulgaria and Romania faced problems in the fields of corruption and threats to the independence of the judiciary

motions.motion: THBT political candidates from minority religious backgrounds should not use their religious identity as an election campaign strategy.
THR the democratization of financial information and financial advice
motions.infoslide: (1) Information democratization refers to the current trend of increased citizen creation, distribution, exhibition, and curation of information. (2) Financial information refers to data and insight related to an individual's or organization's financial situation, including income, expenses, assets, liabilities, investments, and economic trends. Financial advice, on the other hand, involves the interpretation and application of this information to provide guidance or recommendations.

motions.motion: THBT an international body should have control over all water resources on Earth
THBT the feminist movement should oppose policies that incentivise people to get married
THW lift economic sanctions on the Taliban
motions.infoslide: Since 1999, various bodies such as the United Nations, the European Union, and the United States have placed sanctions on the Taliban and Afghanistan more broadly. They fall generally into two categories: economic sanctions, like the suspension of developmental aid and the widespread freezing of government assets, and non-economic sanctions like arms embargos and travel bans.

motions.motion: THBT developing countries should nationalise all mining and resource extraction industries
THBT countries with public healthcare systems should not subsidize life-extending treatments for the elderly or those with terminal illnesses.
motions.infoslide: For the purposes of this debate, life-extending treatment refers to medical interventions that prolong the duration of life without curing the underlying medical conditions. Examples include dialysis, mechanical ventilation, and clinically assisted nutrition.

motions.motion: THW would accept the contract to play in the Saudi Pro League
motions.infoslide: You are a young talented rising star in one of the top four European football leagues (Germany, Spain, Italy, and England). You have just been offered a multi-million dollar contract by a club in the emerging Saudi Pro League, and it is substantially higher compensation than any other contract in a European league. The Saudi Pro League is the national football league in Saudi Arabia.

motions.motion: This house prefers a minimalist constitution instead of an extensive constitution
motions.infoslide: A 'minimalist' constitution is a brief text that covers essential aspects of the regulation of the State such as the regulation of its bodies (legislative, executive, judicial, etc.) but without going into greater detail. An 'extensive' constitution tends to have more detailed regulations regarding economic, social and cultural rights.

motions.motion: THS the formation of the AMF
motions.infoslide: The Asian Monetary Fund (AMF) was proposed to be a multilateral alternative to the International Monetary Fund (IMF) and the dominance of the US dollar. The AMF serves a similar purpose to the IMF in monitoring economic risks and providing financial assistance if necessary with the main difference being the exclusion of the West in the AMF. The AMF was abandoned due to staunch Western opposition but has seen a revival due to a public endorsement by Malaysian Prime Minister, Anwar Ibrahim

motions.motion: THP a world in which religions do not emphasize self-deprivation (e.g. ritual fasting, asceticism, voluntary poverty)

THBT the feminist movement should advocate for girlboss feminism
motions.infoslide: Girlboss feminism emphasizes individual empowerment and success within existing capitalist structures, often portraying entrepreneurship as a means of liberation for women. It tends to prioritize personal achievement over systemic change, advocating for women's advancement in business and leadership roles

motions.motion: THR the trend of celebrities becoming more involved in politics i.e. Kanye’s Presidential Campaigns, Dua Lipa's support of Albania nationalism, celebrities endorsing politicians etc.

motions.infoslide: Internet vigilantism is the act of using the internet by ordinary individuals rather than the legal system to seek justice. This includes leaking information, publishing lists of perpetrators, and hacktivism.

motions.motion: THR the use of the "troubled jock" trope in popular media
motions.infoslide: The "troubled jock" is a high school rugby player who engages in typical high school hierarchies such as bullying, but secretly experiences external moral or identity conflicts (e.g sexuality, feminine interests, abusive households) which motivate their behaviour. Examples include Dave Karofsky from Glee, Jess from Gilmore Girls, and Jake Perry from 13 Reasons Why

motions.motion: This house regrets the notion of 'sisterhood' in feminism.
motions.infoslide: Sisterhood is the belief that women have a collective sense of unity, purpose, and solidarity. This includes the belief that women should always support each other.

motions.motion: HR the narrative that personal suffering is "empowering"
THO the use of cultural relativism in post-colonial law making
motions.infoslide: Cultural relativism is the view that morals must be assessed according to their cultural contexts. Examples in lawmaking include Asian Values, the legitimization of religious institutions, and adherence to sharia law.

motions.motion: THS the rise of situationships
motions.infoslide: A situationship is defined as a non-committed relationship where individuals are not necessarily in a labelled relationship. This is not inclusive of open relationships

motions.motion: TH, as a working mother of multiple children in an Asian country, would prioritize their resources for their most talented children as opposed to distributing resources equally
THR the ruling of the WTO appellate to uphold the use of Article XXI in the GATT
motions.infoslide: The General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT) is a legal agreement between many countries, whose overall purpose was to promote international trade by reducing or eliminating trade barriers such as tariffs or quotas-- this is the general foundation and guideline of the World Trade Organization (WTO). However, under Article XXI of the GATT (also known as the National Security Exception), member states can increase trade restrictions if it is done in the interest of national security. In 2018, Donald Trump put tariffs on steel and aluminum imports to the US, claiming that this was done in the interest of national security (i.e. invoking Article XXI), and therefore allowed under WTO rules. China, Canada, the EU, and other nations have all filed cases against this policy in the WTO, while Saudi Arabia, India, and Russia have supported the US and have since used the "interest of national security" to raise trade barriers in their respective countries. After a three-year court ruling (from the WTO appellate), the WTO continued to uphold Article XXI

motions.motion: THBT the scientist should not publish the information.
motions.infoslide: You are a scientist, working alone one night, you discover that humanity will inevitably end in exactly 5 years. You are alone in the lab and the odds of someone else discovering this information are non-existent.

motions.motion: THR NYU's decision to fire the professor
motions.infoslide: Maitland Jones was a widely respected organic chemistry professor at NYU. He was fired from his job after 82/350 of his students signed a petition against him complaining about his class. The students stated that the classes were too difficult, and that they received little help and sympathy during the pandemic in the form of extra credit which would ruin the chances of students especially planning to pursue med school

motions.infoslide: With budgetary aid, funds are given directly to a recipient country's government. This is independent from project aid (funds directed towards a particular relief project) and central bank aid.

motions.infoslide: For the purposes of this debate, there exists a technology that can accurately measure how successful rehabilitation has been and determine whether someone will recommit crimes in the future

They are all highly competitive, with the highest acceptance rate in 2022 being 6.9% (Cornell University) and the lowest being 3.2% (Harvard University).

motions.motion: TH, as an incredibly skilled gamer trapped in the virtual world, W attempt to beat the tower.
motions.infoslide: The world of Sword Art Online (SAO) is one where a selected few people were arbitrarily trapped in a popular VR video game, where if you die in the game you die in real life. The bodies of the people trapped here are kept alive and they have no risk of dying from outside causes. Since being trapped, a society has formed around the tower that allows people to live a life in the virtual world. However, there is no way to contact the outside world and see the people outside the game. There only way to escape the game is to clear a 100 floor tower, filled with monsters and dangerous bosses. These floors can be cleared with other players trapped within the game, but there is no risk of the monsters escaping.

motions.motion: THS Just Stop Oil
motions.infoslide: Just Stop Oil is a British-based environmental organisation that has gained notoriety in the media for their use of "non-violent civil resistance" tactics in their advocacy. Such tactics include Just Stop Oil members sitting on the racetrack during an F1 race, blocking highways with marches, and blocking access roads to critical oil infrastructure, which disrupts the oil refinery process. Some of Just Stop Oil's objectives include demanding the UK Government stop licensing any new oil, coal and gas projects in the country.

motions.motion: This House, as the journalist, would continue to keep his identity a secret
motions.infoslide: You are a journalist who has recently uncovered secret, indisputable evidence that a long-thought-to-be-dead war criminal who was involved in atrocities in a significant conflict, is still alive today. However, you find further proof that they have now built a positive life around helping the new community in which he resides, where he assumes a completely new identity. You have knowledge that they may be executed if they were found, caught and extradited.

motions.motion: THR the rise of replica fashion
motions.infoslide: Replicas, or "reps", are pieces of clothing (or other replicable items) that imitate the designs of established brands. Also known as knockoffs, this clothing is not authentically produced. Reps have grown in popularity in recent years, with websites such as Pandabuy increasing demand for reps in Western markets.

motions.infoslide: Collectivism is a kind of ethics based on people being a group. Its opposite is individualism which is based on people being individuals. Collectivists focus on what's good for a whole group. Individualists focus on what's good for each person.

Like most wealthy individuals, you have been western educated in your youth, but have since spent all your time in NK. Although you must still abide by the laws of North Korea, you have monetary freedom to access luxuries, higher education, and sometimes foreign goods and services as part of the North Korean elite. You are an only child to two parents, and have no other family members, but as all humans do, you and your parents may have business acquaintances, friends, and far removed relatives. In the past you have never had the opportunity to escape the nation at all. In this current moment you have a unique, once-in-a- lifetime opportunity to defect to South Korea with your family with the certainty of complete physical safety.

motions.infoslide: BRICS is a group of emerging economies created to coordinate its members’ economic and diplomatic policies, found new financial institutions, and reduce dependence on the U.S. dollar, such as through establishing the New Development Bank (NDB) and Contingent Reserve Arrangement (CRA) as alternatives to Bretton-Woods institutions. BRICS current has 9 member states: Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa, Iran, Egypt, Ethiopia, and the United Arab Emirates. BRICS recently offered invitations to 13 nations to become partner countries, including four in ASEAN (Thailand, Indonesia, Malaysia and Vietnam).

motions.motion: THS Western universities terminating Confucius Institutes on their campuses
motions.infoslide: Confucius Institutes (CIs) are educational partnerships between Chinese universities and universities abroad. CIs are funded and arranged by the Chinese Ministry of Education, with the stated aim of promoting Chinese language and culture, supporting international teaching of the Chinese language, and facilitating cultural exchanges. Recently there has been a trend of Western universities (e.g. in the US, Sweden, the Netherlands) closing the CIs on their campuses, citing concerns about potential political influence or limiting academic freedom.

Recently, the Wagner group has mounted a mutiny against Putin, marching armed forces into the country, stopping only 200km from Moscow. The head of the mutiny, Yevgeny Prigozhin, was greeted warmly by Russian citizens and met as well with strong anti-Putin sentiments. The mutiny was eventually stopped by a deal brokered by Lukashenko, President of Belarus and the Wagner group walked away unscathed from any Russian retaliation

In the event the loan is not paid back on time, additional fees are imposed on the debtor and one's credit score may be detrimentally affected.

After their contract expiry in 2023, all 4 members of BLACKPINK renewed their contracts with YG Entertainment for group activities; each member has since signed with a different record label for solo activities.
