TH regrets the use of heroic national myths in school history
motions.infoslide: Heroic national myths are simplified, celebratory stories taught as part of national history that present the nation’s founders, leaders, or defining events as uniquely virtuous or destined for greatness, while downplaying wrongdoing, complexity, or contested perspectives. Ie, Malaysia/Singapore founding-era development stories that compress leftist currents and minority grievances, Japan’s minimisation of the Nanjing Massacre in certain textbooks and Russia’s “Great Patriotic War” hagiography that downplays Stalinist repression.

motions.motion: TH prefers art that offends over art that persuades to achieve social change
motions.infoslide: “Art that offends” is art that shocks on purpose to breaks taboos or insult strongly held beliefs to force public attention and debate. ie, AI Weiwei's middle finger photo towards Tiananmen to challenge authority and say citizens should judge power and not worship it or Andre Serrano's Piss Christ. "Art that persuades” is art that wins people over by telling clear stories, empathy and simple messages to build broad support. ie. Civil Rights photography displaying images of peaceful protesters being attacked by police dogs and fire hoses. These pictures build empathy and make the problem easy to understand, helping ordinary people and lawmakers support new protections and fairer laws.

motions.motion: TH Prefers a World where failure is celebrated instead of being frowned upon
THW regulate commercial fortune-telling
THBT media should not report acts of terrorism
THR the rise of Hollywood's alterations to films for Chinese Box Offices
motions.infoslide: Hollywood have been altering the films as a strategy to attract more Chinese audiences. Examples include Marvel tentpoles Black Widow, Eternals and Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings from Disney, Space Jam: A New Legacy from Warner Bros., and Venom: Let There Be Carnage and Spider-Man: No Way Home from Sony.

motions.motion: TH, as a progressive voter, would choose not to vote for a progressive candidate who is part of a political dynasty.
THR the emphasis on bipartisanship in policymaking
THS the use of blind voting in national elections.
motions.infoslide: For the context of this debate, blind voting refers to an electoral system where ballots do not include the names of politicians and partylists and instead are replaced by a number of policy and platform preferences that voters can get to choose from.

motions.motion: THR the rise of teenagers at the forefront of social movements (e.g Greta Thunberg, Malala Yousafzai, the Parkland students)
THR the narrative that mainstream social movements should refrain from being aggressive and antagonistic in fighting injustice
TH, as the feminist movement, would actively encourage women to take a "hate all men" approach to their interactions
motions.infoslide: The "hate all men" narrative refers to having an inherent prejudice or bias against men, especially through harboring suspicion towards their intentions.

motions.motion: THR Covid Zero policy of the Chinese Government
motions.infoslide: China's zero-COVID policy is an attempt to prevent any community spread of the coronavirus. To achieve this goal the country has enacted some draconian measures, implementing a strategy of using strict, targeted lockdowns and mass testing.

motions.motion: THBT the Quad should pursue a more aggressive stance against China
motions.infoslide: Known as the 'Quadrilateral Security Dialogue' (QSD), the Quad is an informal strategic forum comprising four nations, namely United States of America (USA), India, Australia and Japan. One of the primary objectives of the Quad is to work for a free, open, prosperous and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.

motions.motion: THBT the Marcos Administration should seek to pivot its foreign policy to the US over China
THBT music streaming services (Apple Music, Spotify etc) does more harm than good to artists
THR the rise of individuals sharing their personal lives on social media
THP that speech and user content on social media be regulated by the government as opposed to set independently by the platforms
THR the rise of megacities
THBT developing countries with high rare earth mineral reserves, should set up their own state-owned enterprises rather than rely on multinational corporations to extract them.
THBT it is legitimate for scientists to exaggerate the consequences of climate change in their research
THW stop public funding of space exploration
THW deny people who practises unhealthy lifestyle (i.e smoking, unhealthy eating) access to public healthcare
THBT media aimed at children should reject the "happily ever after" model and show a more realistic picture
motions.infoslide: The "happily ever after" model refers to the plot device that ends the show with a happy ending that benefits the main characters after a difficult situation or circumstance throughout the show.

motions.motion: THP a world in which religions preach that the outcomes in your life are predetermined rather than preaching that your choices influence your outcomes
motions.infoslide: Many religions advocate for the divine destiny. This is seen in for example, the Christian scriptures and Islamic belief in al-qadar. That is, God has a predetermined plan for each one of his creation.

motions.motion: THBT the ownership of globally significant environmental spaces (eg: Amazon rainforest, Mount Everest, Great Barrier reef etc) should be transferred to an International Organisation (Eg: UN)
motions.infoslide: Globally significant Environmental spaces refer to areas around the world which provide important ecosystem services. For instance, in addition to its biodiversity, the Amazon Rainforest helps to stabilise the global climate by absorbing sizable amounts of CO2 and releasing oxygen. Simillarly the Himalayan Mountain range is an important source of fresh water for many surrounding regions. The Great barrier reef is the most extensive coral reef on Earth, with a unique marine ecosystem. These are just some examples

motions.motion: TH as a state with an ageing population would opt for policies that increase immigration (e.g. giving out more visas) rather than ones that encourage citizens to have more children (e.g. offering tax benefits to families that have children)
motions.infoslide: Population ageing refers to an increase in a mean and median age of a country's population caused by an increase in life expectancy and a reduction in birth rates. As a result an increasingly larger portion of the country's population is comprised of the elderly, while the proportion of children and young adults are declining.

motions.motion: THBT government funded scientific research grants should only be awarded to projects based on the scale of social impacts and immediate foreseeability of results (e.g. less than 5 years to obtain results)
THBT that the Feminist Movement should actively promote norms of stable monogamous relationship
motions.infoslide: Monogamy is a relationship with only one partner at a time, rather than multiple partners (ie: marriage or long term relationship between two individuals)

motions.motion: THO the narrative that a university education will lead to a better quality of life
THBT the project of sexual liberation has failed the feminist movement
THW implement the Environmental Credit Score System
motions.infoslide: An Environmental Credit Score (ECS) is a metric designed to evaluate and quantify the environmental impact of an individual based on key factors such as their carbon footprint, resource consumption, waste management, energy efficiency, pollution and emissions. The ECS would be used for, but not limited to, employment decisions, consumer choices, policy-making, etc.

motions.motion: THS the dominance of China in innovating sustainable technology (e.g. EVs, renewable energy)
THW allow employees to relinquish benefits (e.g. leaves, year-end bonuses, HMO) in exchange for a higher monthly salary
THBT the state should aggressively fund the creation and adoption of AI companions for childcare purposes as opposed to traditional child care facilities
motions.infoslide: AI companion bots are AI systems designed to interact with humans, providing support and companionship. In childcare, they can be used to assist with educational activities, offer emotional support, monitor safety, and help manage daily routines.

motions.motion: In areas with high crime rates, THS the creation of local vigilante groups (e.g. Bakassi Boys in Nigeria)
THS the rise of true crime entertainment
motions.infoslide: True crime is a nonfiction genre that combines investigative journalism and narrative storytelling to report on and describe a real-life crime event to the public. Examples include 'Serial', a podcast that investigated the 1999 murder of Hae Min Lee as well as the investigations and legal proceedings that followed.

motions.motion: As ASEAN, This House Would actively reject the presence of AUKUS
motions.infoslide: AUKUS is a trilateral security pact between Australia, the United Kingdom, and the United States. Their current objectives are to reduce China's power in Southeast Asia through means such as, but not limited to, assisting Australia in acquiring nuclear-powered submarines. ASEAN is an economic union comprising of Southeast Asian nations: Brunei, Cambodia, Indonesia, Laos, Malaysia, Myanmar, the Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, and Vietnam.

motions.motion: THO the involvement of religious institutions and leaders in protest and revolutionary movements against authoritarian regimes (e.g. Liberation Theology in Latin America, Muslim Brotherhood in Egypt)
THS granting megacities autonomous control over their economic and social policy (e.g. immigration, health, criminal justice).
motions.infoslide: Megacities are cities that are densely populated financial powerhouses in a country. These cities are rapidly growing and attract a lot of domestic and international investment. Examples include Beijing, Mumbai, New York City, Paris or London. Sometimes these cities are granted special permission to have different laws for business and trade than the rest of the country, but national taxation policy (e.g. people living there paying taxes to the federal government) remains under the control of the federal government.

motions.motion: THW require all political candidates to pass a test before running for office
motions.infoslide: For the purposes of this debate, the test for politicians will determine their ability to make good decisions in governing a country. The test will include aspects of economics, international relations and governance. It will be scored by a panel of experts who are well versed in the respective fields.

motions.motion: THS the aggressive incentivization of "Menara Gading" in rural areas.
motions.infoslide: Menara Gading is a malay proverb that means higher education. Families from low socioeconomic backgrounds, especially in rural areas prioritize the pursuit of higher education for their children in cities and abroad even if it comes at the cost of the family's own wellbeing.

motions.motion: TH Regrets the narrative of “happily ever after” for queer romantic relationships within popular media.
THO the narrative of predestination
motions.infoslide: Predestination is a popular concept employed in multiple religions such as Islam (Al-Qadr) and Christianity. It is the belief that all events happening are occuring due to a plan and that God has foreseen and willed this specific choice for you. It also looms into ideas that God specificly chooses to offer salvation to specific individuals and denies that salvation to others as well.

motions.motion: THS the creation of an education system that suggests that Human Beings are neither good nor evil as opposed to teaching kids that people are inherently good or that all people have good in them.

In light of a worsening economy, This House Supports the introduction of monthly withdrawal limits in developing countries with progressively worsening economies (Countries such as Pakistan, Sri Lanka, Lebanon, etc).
THS acts of vigilantism in areas of high crime
motions.infoslide: Vigilantism is the act of preventing, investigating and punishing perceived offenses and crimes without legal authority. If you're a vigilante, you might take the law into your own hands by trying to catch or punish someone in your own way. If you wrestle jaywalkers down to the ground, you might be called a vigilante.

motions.motion: THR the popularity of true crime entertainment
motions.infoslide: True crime is a nonfiction literary, podcast, and film genre in which the author examines an actual crime and details the actions of real people associated with and affected by criminal events. The crimes most commonly include murder; about 40 percent focus on tales of serial killers.

motions.motion: In light of recent events in Iran, THR the practice of certain feminist groups in openly denouncing their faith and religious practices (Openly throwing/burning their Headscarfs in Iran and India, etc).
This House Regrets the practice of banning highly popular and controversial Social Media Personalities such as Andrew Tate.
THBT that there isn't, and has never been, any justification for war
THS the right to die
THBT affirmative action policy does more harm than good in achieving the goal of equality for marginalised communities
THBT feminist movements in developing countries should not advocate for sexual liberation
motions.infoslide: Sexual liberation refers to the ideology and movement that seeks to free individuals from societal, cultural, and institutional restrictions placed on their sexual expression, behaviors, and choices. It emphasizes personal autonomy, consent, and the right to engage in consensual sexual activities without fear of judgment, stigma, or discrimination.

motions.motion: THBT social media platforms should be held legally accountable for heavily lopsided algorithms that promote echo chambers on issues involving national security and justice (e.g. elections, Amber Heard v Depp trial)
THBT governments should prioritize income redistribution as a means to alleviate poverty over promoting economic incentives for wealth creation
THW allow people sentenced to life in prison to choose the death penalty instead.
THBT Ophelia does not owe reprations to Evangelince for the damages of the accident
motions.infoslide: Evangeline was on her way to a buisness flight when she was involved in a car accident leaving her disabled for life. Ophelia, the driver who hit her, did so completely unintentionally, and have already had her licence revoked making her no longer a threat to any future victims. The day after the accident, it turns out the flight Evangeline was supposed to catch crashed, leaving no survivors. Had Evangeline avoided the accident and made it on time for the flight, she would die as well.

motions.motion: THS the decline of organised religion in favour of new age mysticism.
This house would prefer characters to be explicitly non-queer than made post-textually queer. (For example, Dumbledore, or Lando Calrissian.)
THR regrets the market’s erosion of the nation state
This House supports the use of conscious non-consent by medical professionals in emergency medicine
motions.infoslide: Conscious non-consent is a practice in which medical professionals acknowledge that a patient has refused a particular treatment or procedure in the past but proceed to administer said treatment or procedure anyway because it is necessary to preserve the patient’s life (e.g. blood transfusions, resuscitation).

motions.motion: This house, as a South African, citizen would boycott Moja Love
motions.infoslide: Moja Love is a South African general entertainment channel produced by Siyaya Media Network for DStv that was launched in 2018. In the past few years, the channel had received a lot of criticism because majority of their reality shows demonstrate a lot of violence and dysfunction in the black community. These are shows like Isencane Lengane, Rea Tsotella, Teenage pregnancy etc.

motions.motion: This house believes that retaliatory tarrifs against the U.S are in the long term interest of developing economies
TH as the African feminist movement would reject Chimamanda Ngozi-Adiche as a feminist icon
motions.infoslide: In 2017, Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie said that the experiences of transgender women, who are born with the societal privileges accorded to men, are different from those of cisgender women, and by extension suggested that trans women are not real women. She has since sustained and defended those views, stating, "I have nothing to apologise for."

motions.motion: THS the narrative from Nietzsche that God is Dead
motions.infoslide: The quote "God is dead. God remains dead. And we have killed him." is a famous statement from Friedrich Nietzsche's The Gay Science, published in 1882.

motions.motion: This house believes that the International Criminal Court should be allowed to prosecute individuals for crimes committed in failed states without the consent of that state's government
Assuming you are approached to coach, TH would decline the offer.
motions.infoslide: In September 2024, the KZN Head Coaches and 16 others resigned, citing alleged racism by board members, discrimination against speakers from low-income backgrounds—including capping the number of public school students selected—and the board’s refusal to discipline coaches who behaved inappropriately around school children. This mass resignation prompted a nationwide search for new coaches.

motions.motion: THO the narrative that dreams have meaning
THS major financial institutions accepting cryptocurrencies as collateral
motions.infoslide: In late 2025, JP Morgan, a leading global financial institution providing investment banking, asset management, and financial services introduced a new policy to start accepting cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin and Ethereum as collateral for institutional clients. This marked a major shift in integrating digital assets into traditional finance methods.

motions.motion: THS characters rejecting their prophesied destinies.
motions.infoslide: In many fictional works, characters receive prophecies predicting their future roles or fates. Examples include Harry Potter, who is told he must face Voldemort to save the wizarding world; Percy Jackson, prophesied to undertake quests involving gods and monsters; and Frodo Baggins in The Lord of the Rings, tasked with destroying the One Ring. These prophecies often arise during times of crisis, where the world faces great threats such as war or apocalypse.

motions.motion: In times of low followership, THBT religious institutions should abandon the practice of religious surveillance
motions.infoslide: For the purpose of this debate religious surveillance is a persistent monitoring of the behaviour of followers within a religion. This exists as a heightened expectation to follow a prescribed timeline and bio markers of age in reaching rites of passages such as marriage, child bearing etc. This occurs both in secular and nonsecular states.

motions.motion: THW choose the husband over the first love
motions.infoslide: You have died, and are in the afterlife you find yourself face to face with the two great loves of your life. The first is your husband: your most recent love, with whom you spent the majority of your life and had children. He passed away only a few months before you, and remained by your side until the very end. The second is your first love: the person you once planned to spend your entire life with, who tragically died young while the two of you were still together, and waited for you in the afterlife for 60 years.

motions.motion: THBT African states should heavily prioritise ‘Looking East’ rather than ‘Looking West’
motions.infoslide: Looking West’ prioritises trade and partnerships with Europe and the United States, along with liberal democratic values like human rights and market- based governance. ‘Looking East’ prioritises economic ties with East Asian countries such as Japan and China, alongside state-led development models and more pragmatic or centralised political approaches.

motions.motion: THBT the Squid Game system is morally superior to real-world capitalism
motions.infoslide: In the fictitious survival competition Squid Game, 456 impoverished people willingly enter a closed system to participate in a number of kid-friendly games with lethal outcomes. After being fully informed that elimination in the game results in death, each participant, who has accrued enormous debt in the real world, consents to play. Each round's survivors compete until one person wins a substantial cash prize that is paid for by the deaths of every other competitor. An organised authority oversees the system, ensuring that all outcomes are solely based on in-game performance, that all players have the same starting conditions (food, shelter, and rules), and that all players are subject to the same regulations.

motions.motion: THO the consumption of celebrity culture
THS Progressive Federalism
motions.infoslide: Progressive federalism is the pursuit of progressive policy goals using the state governments as opposed to the US Federal system ie. National govenment

motions.motion: THR “Wise Elder” narratives
motions.infoslide: Literature, folklore, and cultures all around the world commonly hold conceptions of elders as inherently wise and knowledgeable (e.g., societal norms of elder respect, the wise old woman/man in fables, literary archetypes, etc.)

motions.motion: This House opposes the pressure placed on prominent Muslims in Western Liberal Democracies (WLDs) to condemn problems in Muslim countries e.g. terrorism, women's rights, LGBT discrimination
THR the predominant focus of history education in Southern Africa on colonial struggle over pre-colonial history.
THBT the sex worker rights movement should advocate for a narrative of sexual empowerment as opposed to victimization.
TH, as countries from the Global South, would reject external sources of financing and pursue self-reliance.
THS a global pause on CRISPR-Cas9 technology
motions.infoslide: CRISPR-Cas9 is a unique technology that enables geneticists and medical researchers to edit parts of the genome by removing, adding or altering sections of the DNA sequence of humans, animals and plants. E.g. For humans and animals, edits can be made to the DNA of somatic cells (body cells except for sperm and eggs), sex cells (sperm and eggs), zygotes (fertilised eggs) and embryos.

motions.motion: This house believes that Western states should support local militia groups (e.g., funding, intelligence-sharing, weapons-sharing, training) over direct intervention in other countries
THS the implementation of austerity measures instead of stimulus packages in times of economic crisis.
TH, as the IOC, would ban Israel from the 2024 Paris Olympics
This House prefers living in Arcadia to contemporary liberal democracy.
motions.infoslide: Arcadia is a state that is divided into semiautonomous zones, organized on the basis of political philosophy. In Arcadia for example, civilisation is composed of a libertarian community with a fully deregulated economy and low taxes, an anarcho-primitivist community where people live in a state of nature with no laws, and more. There is a neutral central entity whose only duties are to prevent communities of Arcadia from causing harm to each other, ensuring freedom to move from one community to another, and preventing unwanted ideological contamination. Information about each zone is disemminated and assumed to be accessible to all.

motions.motion: THBT it is justified to create a Sikhs state, as demanded by the Khalistan movement.
motions.infoslide: The Khalistan Movement is a movement by Sikhs who advocate for the creation of an independent state called Khalistan in India. It gained momentum in the 1980s in Punjab, India, where Sikhs faced persecution as a minority religious group. There is tension between the Indian government and the most vocal Sikhs of the movement who reside abroad.

motions.motion: THO the research and development of brain emulations
motions.infoslide: For the purposes of this debate, a brain emulation is a technology that creates a functional replica of a human brain, or parts of it. It replicates the function or activity of the part of the brain that it replaces, such as by processing information and responding to stimuli in the same way as a biological brain, or replicating thoughts and memories.

motions.motion: THW grant minority groups that have suffered systematic human rights violations by their government the exclusive right to determine the model of the transitional government through a binding referendum.
THR the romanticisation of homegrown players in sports
motions.infoslide: "Homegrown player" refers to an athlete who was either born and raised in the club's hometown or developed through the club’s youth academy, typically having joined the system at a young age e.g Lebron James with the Cleveland cavaliers, Lionel Messi with Barcelona etc"

motions.motion: This House supports the use of coordinated non-compliance by coalition partners in South Africa’s Government of National Unity to defend equitable power-sharing and coalition integrity.
This house, as WWE, would induct Chris Benoit in the WWE Hall of Fame
motions.infoslide: The late Chris Benoit was a former professional wrestler who worked for the WWE. During his career, Chris Benoit held 22 championships between the industry's top promotions and won the World Heavyweight Championship in the main event of Wrestlemania XX, the largest wrestling show at the time. In June 2007, Chris Benoit murdered his wife, his son, and himself. A medical report of Chris Benoit's brain revealed he had suffered from depression and chronic traumatic encephalopathy (CTE). It was suggested that these resulted from numerous concussions Chris Benoit had sustained by performing moves that the WWE permitted him to do. Since then, WWE has removed, censored, and banned all mentions of Chris Benoit from their networks and media.

motions.motion: This House, as a government of a monetarily sovereign state in the Global South, would follow Modern Monetary Theory over traditional fiscal conservatism in setting fiscal policy.
motions.infoslide: The Modern Monetary Theory suggests that governments with their own currency can spend more freely, using deficits to boost the economy and societal welfare, the only constraint to spending being inflation. Fiscal conservatism prioritizes balanced budgets, low debt, and limited spending to protect long- term stability.

motions.motion: THO the widespread media comparison between the political rise of Donald Trump and Adolf Hitler
THBT liberal democracies should adopt an official posture of religious indifference rather than religious neutrality.
motions.infoslide: For the purposes of this debate religious neutrality means the state treats all religions equally. Religious indifference means the state does not engage with religion at all, not privileging, not recognizing, not accommodating

motions.motion: This House prefers a world in which Germany had won the First World War
motions.infoslide: For the purposes of this debate, a 'German victory' refers to a counterfactual scenario in which the Central Powers, led by Germany, defeat the Allied Powers and dictate a peace settlement favorable to their interests. This could include territorial acquisitions, economic dominance in Europe, the preservation of the German Empire and monarchy, and the weakening or collapse of colonial powers such as Britain and France.

motions.motion: THS media houses including clauses in marriage journey coverage contracts that grant them exclusive rights to cover a potential divorce
motions.infoslide: Marriage journey coverage contracts are agreements between media houses and individuals—typically public figures—that grant the media exclusive rights to document and broadcast key events in a couple’s relationship, often including the proposal, traditional ceremonies, wedding celebrations, and in some cases, their post-marriage developments. These contracts are designed not just to capture the romance and spectacle of weddings but also to secure long-term content opportunities tied to the couple's public narrative. For example, Becoming Mrs Jones, the docu-series covering Minnie Dlamini’s lavish wedding journey, was reportedly sold to Vuzu/Showmax for R6 million and became the highest-rated program on Vuzu Amp. Similarly, Somizi & Mohale: The Union offered an exclusive, multi-part look into Somizi Mhlongo and Mohale Motaung’s extravagant traditional and white weddings, breaking streaming records on Showmax.

motions.motion: THBT the Dalai Lama should not reincarnate
motions.infoslide: The Dalai Lama is the highest spiritual leader in Tibetan Buddhism and is traditionally believed to reincarnate after death. The current Dalai Lama, now in his late 80s and living in exile, has suggested he may choose not to reincarnate. The Chinese government, which claims sovereignty over Tibet, has declared that it will oversee and approve any future reincarnation, asserting legal authority over the process.

motions.motion: THS the use of liberation theology as a framework for social justice movements in predominantly religious developing states
motions.infoslide: Liberation theology blends Christian doctrine with political activism, particularly in Latin America and Africa, advocating for the poor and oppressed.

motions.motion: This House would treat repeated premeditation of a crime in lucid dreams as grounds for preventive detention.
motions.infoslide: Lucid dreaming refers to a state in which individuals are consciously aware that they are dreaming and can intentionally control the content and direction of their dreams. In this world, all individuals possess the ability to lucid dream and recall them in vivid detail. Additionally, technology allows for the accurate documentation and analysis of lucid dreams—similar to reviewing diaries or surveillance footage.

motions.motion: This House would endorse the practice of audiences actively creating and promoting imposed readings of popular Hollywood productions.
motions.infoslide: James Gunn's 'Superman' film, released on July 11, 2025, has garnered significant social media attention, with some creators drawing parallels between its narrative themes and global conflicts, including the ongoing situation in Gaza. This phenomenon, known as 'imposed reading,' occurs when audiences project specific interpretations onto artworks – often political or social justice-oriented – even when authors have not explicitly stated or have actively denied such intentions

motions.motion: THR the South African government's reliance on commissions of inquiry to address systemic issues of corruption and governance failure
THW be "delulu"
motions.infoslide: Being "delulu" or delusional is having unrealistic or overly optimistic fantasies by ignoring reality in favour of wishful thinking.

motions.motion: Assuming feasibility THW imprison all criminals in simulations as opposed to physical prison systems
motions.infoslide: The Simulation Hypothesis proposes that conscious beings could be immersed within an artificial universe embedded within a higher order of reality. This simulation exposes the human being to what an ideal situation would be and what is expected of them by society. It can be used to alter social quirks and behaviours.

motions.motion: THP a world where all children were swapped after birth
THS China’s intellectual property infringement
motions.infoslide: For decades, Chinese companies have faced widespread accusations of Intellectual Property infringement, including the mass production of counterfeit goods imitating well-known brands, and reverse engineering and breaking the patents of global innovations. Examples include Tesla's electric vehicles, Intel's semiconductors, Apple's iPhone and pharmaceutical drugs. This has become particularly prominent in recent decades due to China's rapid economic growth and increased global trade.

motions.motion: THR the narrative that the use of the "Femme Fatale" architype in media is empowering to women
motions.infoslide: Femme fatale (French for fatal woman) refers to a woman who is sexually attractive, seductive and cunning who brings disaster to anyone with whom she is romantically involved with. It is an archetype found within history, folklore, mythology and religious literature. Examples include but not limited to Cleopatra, Medusa, Delilah, Catwoman. This archetype has grown to be a trope across modern writing and modern media with many adopting it as a symbol of empowerment.

motions.motion: THR the hyper sexualisation of woman by female identifying rappers.
This House believes that reversing gender roles in popular films is an ineffective feminist strategy
motions.infoslide: In several recent mainstream films, male characters are placed in roles traditionally occupied by female characters such as being emotionally dependent, sidelined, or requiring rescue, the damsel in distress trope. For example: In Barbie (2023), Ken lacks purpose outside of Barbie and struggles to find autonomy. In The Hunger Games, Peeta Mellark is frequently rescued or protected by Katniss and is often portrayed as emotionally sensitive and physically weaker

motions.motion: This house would significantly subsidize medical treatments and research which extend female fertility
motions.infoslide: Medical treatments that aim to extend female fertility focus on preserving or prolonging a woman’s ability to conceive as she ages. These include procedures such as egg freezing, ovarian tissue preservation, and hormonal therapies that delay menopause or improve egg quality. Research in this field explores areas like stem cell therapy, gene editing, and anti-aging drugs for reproductive organs to slow down or reverse the decline in fertility associated with age. Access to these treatments is often limited by high costs and uneven availability across healthcare systems.

motions.motion: This house believes that the international community should push for increased reliance on the Chinese Yuan

In the event of an eminent planet level destructive event like a collision with a massive asteroid, This house would prioritise the continued preservation of humans with proven intellectual propensity towards the natural sciences over those inclined towards the social sciences
This house supports the implementation of quotas for women in key creative roles in the film industry.
THBT conflict resolution in regions with religious diversity should prioritize empowering local religious leaders as mediators and peacemakers
THBT African governments should not pursue power-sharing agreements between the ruling party and major opposition parties
This House, as developing countries, would institute import bans on food products.
THBT the ongoing process of decolonization should include actively disempowering religions that were imposed on the country during colonialism
THW ban all forms of monetization for social media accounts run by children
TH Welcomes the decline of the USA as a global hegemon
THBT progressive individuals should have a zero tolerance for people with regressive values in their social circle
motions.infoslide: A "Zero Tolerance Policy" in this debate refers to cutting contact with a person to as great of an extent as reasonably possible. In the case of family or essential co-workers, this can look like being civil but never close to them. For a friend, it can mean cutting them off completely.

motions.motion: THS radical environmentalism
motions.infoslide: For the purposes of this debate, 'radical environmentalism refers to the use of aggression or disruption (e.g vandalism, sabotage civil disobedience) in the name of environmentalist campaigns. An example of 'radical environmentalism' was when members of the Just Stop Oil campaign vandalized Vincent van Gogh's "Sunflowers" in the National Gallery and subsequently glued their hands to the wall in 2022.

motions.motion: THR the narrative that feminists should refrain from being antagonistic and aggressive in fighting patriarchy.
TH, as a high achieving student, Would choose to pursue a high-paying risky career over a moderate-paying, stable career
As a charity successfully making the world a better place, THW reject donations from immoral actors
THW grant amnesty to Joseph Kony and other LRA leaders in exchange for full disclosure of the group's activities.
motions.infoslide: From 1987 to 2006, the Lord's Resistance Army (LRA), under the leadership of Joseph Kony, instilled fear in the Central African region. Operating mainly in Uganda, the LRA became infamous for its brutal tactics, including abducting young children to serve as soldiers and sex slaves. Kony's rebels terrorized communities, leaving a trail of destruction in their wake. The LRA's reign of terror led to displacement, loss of life, and profound humanitarian crises. In response to international pressure and regional military efforts, the LRA was eventually forced out of Uganda. However, it continued its atrocities in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and the Central African Republic (CAR), creating a complex and enduring humanitarian challenge that demanded coordinated efforts to address the aftermath of its brutal campaign.

motions.motion: For the purpose of the debate, God is Immortal and All-Powerful. THP a world where God was one of us
THO the trend where founders of companies remain in significant leadership positions (CEO, President, Techno-King, etc.,) in the company for a long time after it has gone public

THBT developed nations should allocate a larger portion of their foreign aid budget towards supporting sustainable agriculture and improving food security in developing countries as opposed to funding peacekeeping missions.
THBT the Democratic Republic of Congo should nationalize its mining industry.
motions.infoslide: Decades of violence plague eastern DR Congo, fueled by a deadly mix of factors. Resource wealth, like gold and coltan, tempts armed groups like M23 to fight for control. Ethnic tensions simmer beneath the surface, further fracturing the region. While offering valuable insights, alternative indicators also face challenges related to data availability, standardization, and subjectivity.

In a 2020 match against Brighton, Manchester United was awarded a penalty after the full-time whistle had already blown. Following a missed handball by Neal Maupay in the final seconds, VAR intervened as players were leaving the pitch. Since the referee hadn't left the field, rules allowed him to restart play. motions.motion: THBT the Ezulwini consensus of 2005 should focus primarily on UNSC expansion as opposed to permanent seats for African representation
motions.infoslide: "The Ezulwini Consensus is Africa’s unified position on reforming the United Nations Security Council. It calls for expanding the Council from 15 members to at least 26 seats to better reflect global realities. A core demand is permanent representation for Africa. The African Union insists that Africa should have at least two permanent seats, with the same rights as existing permanent members, including the veto power such as. In addition to permanent seats, Africa seeks five non-permanent seats, ensuring broader and more consistent African participation in Security Council decision-making. As elections for the UN approach, Africa's campaign for reforms in the UNSC has resurfaced with the Ezulwini consensus as the guiding template. "

THS the adoption of the CBDC in the East African Countries
motions.infoslide: Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) is a digital form of a central bank-issued money. CBDC would be denominated in the same national unit of currency - and serve as legal tender. It would be a direct liability of the National Bank, so it would be solely issued and centrally governed by the Central Bank. In addition, it would be exchangeable one to one (1:1) against other forms of currency, such as physical cash, deposit money (bank balances), and mobile money. Because it is held in electronic form, it would additionally be usable in online or remote payment scenarios.

motions.motion: THP a world where humans had a 'half full' disposition to world where humans have a 'half empty' disposition.
motions.infoslide: For the purpose of this debate, humans can have two dispositions when imagining how things could be different. The 'half empty' disposition involves imagining that things could be better. The 'half full' disposition involves imagining that things could be worse. These dispositions do not solely apply to how things could change in the future, but also to potential counterfactuals. E.g., if they were imagining how their phone might be different, a 'half empty' person would imagine that it could be faster or have better battery life. A 'half full' person would imagine that it could break or be a less technologically advanced model.

motions.infoslide: Common but Differentiated Responsibilities "(CBDR) is a principle in international environmental law that acknowledges that while all countries share a common obligation to protect the environment, their responsibilities vary based on their historical contributions to environmental degradation and their respective capacities to address such issues. This principle is particularly emphasized in the context of climate change, recognizing that developed nations, having contributed more to the problem, should take on greater responsibilities compared to developing countries (which can be excused or given less responsibility in response to climate change in certain circumstances)

motions.motion: THO the practice of Tatemae
motions.infoslide: In Japan, "Honne" refers to a person's true feelings and desires, and "Tatemae" refers contrastingly to the behaviour and opinions one displays in public. The norms around Tatemae are meant to minimise discord, and failing to observe Tatemae might be seen as insulting or aggressive. In some cases, Tatemae leads to outright telling of lies in order to avoid exposing the true inward feelings

motions.infoslide: Keystone species are animals which are disproportionately important to the survival of an ecosystem. For example, bees are a keystone species because their pollination is necessary for the reproduction of certain plants; Pacific salmon are another example, because they are the main food source for many predators when they migrate upriver in the spring

This House Regrets the narrative of Corporate Responsibility.
motions.infoslide: The narrative of Corporate Responsibility highlights how corporate self- interest and the pursuit of wealth lead companies to environmental harm. It stresses that the burden to repair environmental harm is borne by only companies and is based on two premises. The first is Corporate Negligence: the failure of companies to take adequate measures to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions, transition to renewable energy, and adopt sustainable practices, despite knowing the risks and consequences of climate change. The second is Corporate Greed: the story of how corporations prioritize profits over people and the planet, contributing to climate change and its devastating impacts.
